In this fantasy card game, the players play troll bandits competing for the most COINs.

Each turn, a player reveals as many cards ('TRAVELLERS') as there are players. Each traveller has an initiative score and will be assigned to each player - the player with the most SKULLs (see below)receives the highest priority card, and so on.

Each TRAVELLER either gains or costs their player coins, or introduces a new mechanic into the game. The higher the priority, the higher the rewards of a TRAVELLER card, but the higher the chance the card will be negative for the player.

Before each card is assigned, players can assign SKULLs to other players, themselves, or no one at all. This happens simultaneously, and after the cards to be assigned cards have been revealed. I.e. if the highest card is costing you a lot of COIN, and you have the most or are tied for the most SKULLs, you will want to give your SKULL to another player and hope you won't get any more, so the highest priority, negative TRAVELLER card doesn't go to you. On the other hand, you don't want to have much less SKULLs as anybody else, as you will constantly miss the high COIN value TRAVELLERs.

There is also a randomised item shop where players can spend coin for one-time or permanent rule changes (redistributing or removing SKULLs, changing priorities, lessen the impact of negative TRAVELLERs, and so on).

The game ends if one player starts and ends a turn with eight or more skulls, as they get to much attention to stay robbing people.

-description from designer

boardgame: Artificial Life 101: Creating Monsters for Undergrad Students
description:
Each player has to gather sets of resources to build a monster from fantasy or sci-fi as a semester thesis. They only have 15 days (rounds) left.

The players gain victory points by turning in sets of resources described by their thesis cards, which are unique to each player and to be kept secret as long as possible. Each thesis emphasises three of the five available resource categories: Science, Magic, Components, Flesh and Energy.

To gather resources, they have to build a laboratory which transform, splits or fuses resources or teleports them all over the place. The players vie for space and race against time to gather the right material in a chaotic card game with a modular playing field and engine-building elements.

Each turn consists of three phases: In the first phase a player adds a tile to or modifies a tile of their laboratory to give it more functions, like turning certain resources into others, duplicating resources or fusing certain resources into more resources of another kind. A smartly planned lab can set of chain reactions to create lots of the wanted resources from any kind of original resource.

In the second phase, they can choose to either feed a new, randomly determined resource into their lab, or let resources in the lab go into the next stage of a reaction.

In the third phase, they can turn in resources gathered in their lab for points or special action cards, which can help them or hinder other players through various bonuses. Turning in resources for points reveals a players thesis to other players, so they can better try to sabotage their labs.

After fifteen rounds, the player who turned in the most relevant sets for their thesis wins. There are multiple strategies to build a functioning lab with their own risks and rewards, and until the last turn there can often be dramatic changes in the score.

-description from designer

Medieval knights clashed to prove their bravery, courage and to impress rich and (sometimes) beautiful women. The Middle Ages are a relict of the past, but men are still men afterall and they still want to prove their superiority. So, what is the most reliable and effective way to distinguish a nobleman from a peasant? Oh yes! Duel! Instead of wearing full plate armor they wear equally charming elegant suits. Instead of fighting on the tournament arena, they meet in the underground of secret club called The Suits. Join this elite society and fight for your prestige and ...........*

*) Enter your deepest and darkest desire that you want to fight for (or die for, but it wasn't in the agreement)

And remember that the first and last rule of The Suits Club is - you do not talk about The Suits Club.

This season is different. Why? Because this time female characters are entering to the club.

Each player controls one of five available characters. Each character has his own unique deck of cards. By using your wits, tactics, strategies and of course, a little bit of luck you can show your enemies who is the real gentleman. The Suits is a fast-paced, two-person card game steaming with atmosphere and tactical depth.

—description from the designer

San Gimignano is a small city in Tuscany, Italy famed for the 72 medieval towers built by families to exhibit their wealth and influence. In Le Torri di San Gimignano, a.k.a. サン・ジミニャーノの塔, each player represents a noble family in medieval Tuscany who is trying to build more towers that are higher and more beautiful than those of your rival.

Players start with five cards in hand and four cards in a face-up market from a deck that contains five 5s, six 6s, and so on to ten 10s. They draw and swap cards from an open market, looking to lay out cards of the same value (a plain tower) or of rising values in a run (a colorful tower). Players can add to existing towers, too. After building a plain tower, they receive a bonus effect (a donation) based on the rank of that tower. Players can complete towers by setting them aside as an action; they can no longer add to these towers, but each scores an additional 5 points at game's end.

In the final scoring, players score for having the highest tower, for plain towers that are taller than the opponent's tallest tower in the same rank, and for their colorful towers (# of towers x # of floors total in these towers). The player with the highest score wins.

The game also includes a set of noble cards for an advanced game, with the nobles having special effects in the game.

Press the head of Onkel Reje, and he says what kind of food he wants you to give him. Press as many times as you want - but be careful, cause if he farts, you don't get to give him anything!
First player to deliver all his food tokens to Onkel Reje, wins the game.

The game includes a figure of Onkel Reje, the (in)famous character from Danish children's TV, that speaks (and farts) when you press him - a sure winner for children aged 5-8 years.

boardgame: The House and Temple Of The Flying Blades: 1st Dan Expansion
description:
Ninja techniques have improved since the last skirmish. Now the dojos can use a new set of stealth movements and also a new weapon.

Also the House now has a few columns were the ninjas can hide behind.

This expansion works for any number of players (2-5).

-description from publisher

Orcs are ravaging the lands and threatening your caves and families! Can you defeat the ravaging orcs and bring back peace and prosperity to your people?

In Caverna: Frantic Fiends, the second large expansion for Uwe Rosenberg's Caverna: The Cave Farmers, you have to face these hostile plunderers. Luckily, you can either use your proven axes to teach them a lesson or build traps to keep them away from the cave entrances altogether. You can even push them into these traps with stone or bribe them with wood to weaken their strength. Defeating orcs will bring precious rewards, but if you fail to drive them off, all your plans might be in jeopardy.

boardgame: 昆虫ゲッチュ！ (Catch the Bug!)
description:
昆虫ゲッチュ！ (Catch the Bug!) is a game from Japan about kids out on a bug hunt.

Players place square cards on the table, building up a grid of bug cards and squads of kids hunting the bugs. When the tableau is complete, players score for the bugs they have secured.

Players try to get their kids to form intersects around some bugs. This means that only one squad can catch each bug.

The kids are ranked by their skills 1-3 with the older kids better more able, and the bugs are also valued 1-4 and -3. Kids can only catch insects if their individual or combined skills are equal or better than the wisdom of the elusive critters. Catching a risky insect like a hornet scores negative points. If multiple squads are competing, the squad with higher skills in total wins the insect.

As the grid develops, players can place good catches at their own kids, and bad catches onto their opponents. The game is quick and easy to play.

At the finish, the highest total score wins.
user summary
artists: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)

boardgame: ムーチョ de チョーショ (Mucho de Chosho)
description:
ムーチョ de チョーショ (Mucho de Chosho) is a party game from Japan.

One player ('parent') lays out four cards showing different themes or ideas about the person, and states how many of them are lies. The other players may ask questions, and after some deduction, they try to pick which card is a lie. If they get it correct, they score, but if they choose wrong, the parent scores a point.

The game is meant as a communication game, which is a very popular form of social game in Japan. The game works as an ice-breaker, and helps people talk about themselves.

The card text is all in Japanese.
user summary
This expansion map provides a different configuration of routes for the base game, Polynesia.

Unlike the original Kakerlakak game, Mein erstes Kakerlakak is aimed at very young players. The mama cockroach — that is, a HEXBUG — runs through a path that loops on the game board. At the start of a round, each player places one of their "food balls" (a colored marble) in the starting location, then you start mama on the path, with her ideally knocking your food through the holes in the path to feed the baby cockroaches that live in the floor. Whoever first has all their food delivered to the babies wins!

Mein erstes Kakerlakak includes a variant for slightly older children in which players try to collect an assortment of food colors to fill their basket first.

Criss-Cross is a simple roll-and-write game for any number of players. Each player has a 5x5 grid of squares.

Each turn two standard dice are rolled and all players enter the sum in an empty box in their grid; 10, 11, and 12 are entered as 0, 1, and 2 respectively.

After 25 turns the grids are full and each player scores their rows and columns: 2/3/4/5 of the same number score 1/3/6/10 points, and 5 sequential numbers (not necessarily in order) scores 5 points. If a row or column contains multiple scoring combinations (two pairs or a pair and a triple) just add the scores for each combination.

The second version of the game (in Dice Games Properly Explained) gives a few variants:


     For larger groups it suggests entering two different numbers on each sheet to begin with and playing 23 turns.



     In Criss and Cross the rows and columns are totaled separately and you only score the lower of the two numbers.



     Criss versus Cross is for two players only; players use the same grid and take turns entering numbers. One player scores rows and the other columns.

Dubbed as the "New Monopoly", Ma Bell is a Monopoly clone with few major differences. The game features cardboard counters depicting telephones as homage to the name (a nickname for Bell Telephone Co.) used as movers and property markers. The property spaces are named after spoofed titles of real stores and businesses of the late 1970s and early 1980s.

A checkers-like game featured in the Quick Winner Family Fun Games line by Hasbro in the 1970s. The game features 48 checkers in orange and yellow. Players take turns filling in the board except for the center circle to set up. An extra checker is used to stack on one of your players to make an "officer". The ultimate goal of the game is to eliminate all of your opponents officers, or force them to be unable to take a move. Single checkers, or "guards" move one space and can either jump checkers to remove them, or move onto a friendly piece to form a new officer. Officers can move any number of spaces in a single direction, but can only remove other officers.

Players take turns trying to get 120 points to win the round or game. Featuring 3 different sized tops, 2 stands, a transfer arm, and 3 small crowns. On their turn, players spin the largest top on the scoring base and then must cap the top with a crown before spinning the next sized top on the spinning platform. Then, using the magnet in the transfer arm, players must get the top to spin on the crown of the previous top and repeat with the third top and final crown before the largest top stops spinning and dumps all of the pieces off. Each top and crown has a point value for the highest level the player achieves on their turn.

A simple flip/roll and move game featuring a map of central/south Pennsylvania Dutch country. Produced in Glen Rock PA by Avalon Hill and sponsored by Heidi House. The game utilizes a deck of cards telling you how many spaces to move or roll after you read the fact or quote about the people and places of PA Dutch Country. Also included is a booklet of information and a recipe book that when referenced by a card allows special movement.

The planet’s coral reefs are in danger and a group of biologists are taking action to protect them. On a mission to Tiny Reef, they take inventory of the reef animals to make it a sanctuary for marine life.

During a game of Tiny Reef, players draw on their sheet the marine animals they observe, following certain constraints. They earn points by forming groups of Marine animals of the same species and fulfilling some of the goals.

—description from the publisher

密林サーチ (Jungle Search) is a party game from Japan, with players searching online densely forested retailers (Amaz*n) to find items, and then guessing the most expensive.

Players draw cards with various themes, and players use the internet to search for items fitting the starting letters. Players work out which one costs the most to score points.

The game does require internet connection (not provided), but you do get a whiteboard and marker with eraser, and 45 theme cards.
user summary

Swap is played with four standard dice and 12 chips in each of four colors. Players start with one chip of each color and the rest are placed in a row of supplies.

At the start of each round the dice are rolled and assigned to the chip supplies - the color on the left gets the highest die, the next color gets the second-highest, etc. Then each player in turn may trade; they can turn in chips to the supplies and take chips, but they must obey two rules: the total value of the chips taken cannot exceed that of those turned in, and at most one chip of each color can be traded.

After each round the start player moves. The winner is the first player to have two chips of each color. If playing solo the object is to reach this goal in as few turns as possible.

The book versions of the game include variants:


     In Sway Swap the dice are not rolled each round; instead they are rolled all together only at the start of the game. On a player's turn they choose a die to roll, after which they re-order the dice and then may trade.



     In Sellout the die rolling is handled as in Sway Swap, but it uses dice in colors matching the chips instead of ordering the dice. In this version the game continues until there is only one chip left in the market, at which point the player with the most chips wins.

Volume IV in one of the Russell little books card game sets. The game features a deck of cards split into four colored triangles with different numbers on them. The goal is to score enough points to win the game, and play your cards to end the round. On their turn players match the colored sides of the cards so that the numbers add to a multiple of 5 to score points, while matching multiple sides can give a bonus as in other domino-style games like Contack.

Volume V in one of the Russell little books card game sets. A simple matching game using cards featuring various motifs of the old American "wild" west. Players take turns drawing cards in the aim to match pairs of cards and law down for points or hold for a gin-style hand. Players have an odd number of cards in their hands so that when all but 1 card is used in a pair that player can use the single card to end the round by calling "cowboy round-up" where hands are scored.

Volume II in one of the Russell little books card game sets. It features a deck of cards depicting dual numbered dominoes and rules to play a number of basic domino games such as traditional dominoes, domino pairs, and Mexican Train.

Spiria Material Card Game StA is a 2 player card game from Japan, based off a previous trading card game called Spiria Material. This game allows players to begin playing in the Spiria Material world with only 5 hand cards each. The game includes just 18 cards for each player, so players can experience playing with a variety of combinations, without having to buy a whole deck of cards.
user summary
publishers: Spimate Production Committee (スピマテ制作委員会 )

description: A 2 player battling card game in the same vein as Pokemon or Magic the Gathering. A collectible trading card game published in Japan featuring many different sets of booster packs, starter decks, and collector's series of cards. The cards themselves depict monsters, bosses, and characters from the Dragon Quest/Dragon Warrior series of games split into 5 different color groups. Players use a deck of monsters and heroes to control the playing field with the help of item, event, and specialty cards as well.

A trivia game where players try to score 7 points or avoid having 7 negative points. Players open each round by betting how many of 7 questions they can answer. The highest bidder wins or loses points based on their score. Featuring 4 categories, zinger cards for added gameplay, and features predominantly questions on African-American culture and history.

Can be played in teams.

Marked Olds Ogden & Johnson, Wilmington DE

A simple roll and move children's game based on the Disney adaptation of Snow White & the Seven Dwarves. Players spin the spinner and move their Prince Charming token trying to meet up with the sleeping Snow White. The spinner also feature a magic mirror space, where players must use a small reflective space on the board to read the instructions on the backwards mirror cards.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description: Now you can play the excitement of the domed sports arcade games at home. Much like the more famous and popular domed rod/stick hockey, some arcades had push button domed sports like soccer, football, and basketball. This smaller tabletop version features 8 keys per side to pass, dribble, and shoot the ball down the court. Similar in play style to older sports games such as Bas-Kat by Cadaco. Different sports versions exist by a number of small companies, but the most prevalent seems to be by Hong Kong Toys made from the late 70s to early 90s.

Produced by Majik Toys and East Point Sports, Game Flipperz is a double sided game board featuring action based sports games. One side features Pitch & Play Baseball which utilizes ringed marbles as in Rebound as baseballs and a pinball style bat to swing downfield. Players have plastic gates to simulate fielders to prevent the batting player from hitting into the slight wells to score runs. The other side of the board features Fling Football, a traditional paper football style game where players can block out 5 or 10 yard sections of the field with their defense tokens to make it more difficult for the offensive player to make it downfield. It also features a plastic catapult and foam football for kick-offs and field goals.

Pods is an electronic tabletop game produced by Sega. The game features a three-piece console with dual-level light-up sensors and a central sensor with speakers and additional lights.

Pods feature rules for seven different games at three different skill levels, most of which deal primarily with speed or memory. The three sensors detect when a player puts their hand over them and responds by lighting up either the bottom half at a six-inch range or the top half around twelve inches. Each of the games utilized the lights or speakers in the central piece to direct players when and where to activate the sensors.

Dilemma or Dubito is played with two or three decks of standard playing cards. Each player is dealt a hand of eight and the remainder form a draw pile.

On a turn, a player plays one card to their layout and then draws a replacement. A player's layout consists of four lines of cards:


    1. Cards that increase in value
    2. Cards in the same suit
    3. Cards in the same suit that increase in value
    4. Cards of the same rank


These lines are fixed at the start of the game (i.e. a player cannot play to a line and decide later which one it is.)

The game ends when no one can play any longer. Players score one point for each card in line 1, two for each in line 2, etc.

The book versions of the game (German and second English) suggest playing with two decks even with more players and also give some variants:


     In Subito a player may play multiple cards on their turn, replenishing only after all cards are played. (With two players use just one deck.)



     In Judico players do not have hands; instead on a turn a player draws a card and then may play it or discard it. Discarded cards are out of the game. The rules for the lines are relaxed in this version.

Skeptical of the rumors, you and your friends decide to see if that creepy old house on the corner lives up to its reputation.

Finding yourselves locked inside with all manner of horrors, you must fight your way out before the house scares you and your friends to death.

Well, at least they’re your friends for now...

Gain and lose Courage Points as you wander through the house, encountering Scares while gaining protective Totems. But don’t worry, dying of fright isn’t the end. You’ll get to haunt the others to keep them from winning and possibly come back from the dead yourself.

Be the first player to gain all six Totems to beat the house and live to tell the tale!

Quick and easy to learn, light and fun highly competitive game.

This is a hand crafted game, made to order with high quality, durable acrylic components.

—description from the designer

August, 1941. Rommel has driven the Western Desert Force all the way across Cyrenica. But the Tiger Convey has just arrived bringing tanks, airplanes, ammunition and supplies. General Wavell now has what he need to drive the Germans back, and seize initiative on the North African front.

The Player who best executes combined arms stands the best chance - you need to integrate all aspects of the North African Desert Space. The game is played in alternating turns. Each players forces will be under double digits as this is tactical game that includes basics like Line of Sight, Elevation, Opportunity Fire, Indirect Fire and armor overruns!

Operation Battleaxe is the third of Dark City Game's Combat Boots series of tactical wargames. Each piece is a squad or section of infantry, an individual vehicle, gun, or aircraft. The scale of the board is 50 meters per hexagon, and each game turn covers only a few minutes.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Targaryen Unsullied Swordmasters
description:
The Unsullied have one of the most difficult training regimens of any unit. Only about one in three survives to the end. However, those that do are swordsmen of unparalleled skill. Though they look slight compared to most western swordsmen, that does not mean they’re not as strong or effective on the battlefield. When joined with an officer, their skills are only enhanced, trading blow-for-blow with any foe.

The Unsullied Swordsmen unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives Targaryen players an effective melee unit on the field. Their melee attacks hit at the weak points in enemy armor, granting numerous bonuses when they strike. The Officer Unit Attachment adds to their strengths, trading wounds with the enemy when being attacked, making this unit able to dish out wounds both when attacking and on the defensive.

Contents:
13 Miniatures
  ‣ 1 Officer
  ‣ 11 Swordmasters
  ‣ 1 Swordmaster Bannerman
1 Unit Card
1 Attachment Card
1 Movement Tray
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Targaryen Mother of Dragons
description:
The Targaryen faction is almost defined by their unique relationship with dragons as both living war machines and companions. This miniatures set brings the Mother of Dragons, Daenerys Targaryen, to the battlefield accompanied by her young dragons. The dragon units will feature the unique ability to fly over other units as well as breathe fire onto their enemies.

The game box includes a Daenerys Targaryen miniature, 3 juvenile dragon miniatures, and character and tactics cards.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Targaryen Unsullied Pikemen
description:
Adding fearsome weapons to the unshakable Unsullied makes for a truly unbreakable center to any formation. Despite their armor rating a "light" classification in the western sense, and Unsullied spear wall, advancing in lockstep according to doctrines laid down in the Old Empire of Ghis, can move on virtually any position without fear of counterattack from the flanks or even the rear. While such a formation might seem immune to cavalry, a commander would be wise not to let them march forth unsupported, but if any unit can weather a short-term encirclement, it's the Unsullied Pikemen.

Contents:
12 Miniatures
  ‣ 12 Unsullied Pikemen
1 Unit Card
1 Movement Tray
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Game: A Song of Ice & Fire – Tabletop Miniatures Game

description:
The Fifth Column is a precursor of Schotten Totten played with a standard deck of cards. In the basic version each player gets a hand of six cards and two rows of nine cards each are placed on the table to start the players' columns. Two cards are discarded and the rest form a draw pile.

On a turn a player plays to one of their nine columns, claims columns if possible, and draws a card. The three-card column sets are ranked from straight flush down. A column can be claimed even if the opponent's set is incomplete if you can prove there is no way they can beat your set. If a column is completed on both sides and is tied, the player who played last loses it.

The object is to claim five columns.

Some variants are given:


     Ignore straights and straight flushes.



     Each player gets 15 cards at the start and never draws any more.



     Don't place any cards in the columns initially, as in the later game.

The war of the kings is almost over, and few are left alive to win it for the them. The last hope for each side is on the way, but each of them has to hold their ground until their fierce commander arrives. Who can conquer this ever-shifting terrain and not fall victim to their own moves?

In Region, players maneuver their king on the grid to claim free spaces and take back the enemy-occupied spaces by surrounding them, while trying not to be encircled by the enemy. After placing their fifth conquest tile, players can enter their commander into the game. While more powerful, commanders are more restricted in their movement, yet they can turn the tide of the game.

The first player to place all of their tiles wins.

Welcome to the world of Elementra. In this fast-paced game, players take on the roles of Elementra students in their first year of training - learning how their powers work in combination with their dragon companions.

Use your abilities, strategy, and luck to create the most powerful duo of dragon and rider you can to defeat all other challengers.

Limelight is played on a 4x4 grid with the corners cut off using pieces in two colors. White plays first and has 11 pieces, black has 10.

On a turn a player enters a piece on one of the edge spaces, pushing a piece there to the next space and so on. However, pieces may not be pushed off the board.

When all pieces have been played the board will be full. The player with the most pieces in the nine central squares wins.

The game can be played on a larger board with more pieces. If the center region has an even number of spaces and the players tie, the first player wins.

An unavoidable calamity has fallen upon earth, and there is no place to take shelter from the wrath of gods. It's been ages since the last temple was seen. You are summoned as the most adept sorcerers in the world to try building the last temple while competing for the favor of gods — but this is no easy task. You have to build it block by block, infusing each block with magic and hoping that it can withstand what is yet to come.

In Temples, the layout of the game consists of a variable arrangement of block cards in the form of a temple, with each block having a number. You try to play cards with a total equal to the number of each block to either:


    Add blocks with larger numbers to your hand, or
    Claim the building blocks of the temple and increase your score.


The game ends once every block of the temple has been claimed. Whoever has the most claimed blocks is the most renowned sorcerer and wins.

boardgame: Anno 1800: The Power of Gold Mini-Expansion
description:
Macht des Goldes ("Power of Gold") is a mini-expansion consisting of new assignment cards that give new options for gaining and spending gold. It consists of two new assignment cards:

     Dr. Hugo Mercier, who allows players to avoid getting new population cards by spending gold.
     Lady Margaret Hunt, who gives players gold when they tear down a building.


—user summary

Im großen Aufbau-Strategie-Spiel baut jeder Spieler kontinuierlich die eigene Industrie auf, um seine Heimatinsel weiterzuentwickeln. Mit dieser Mini-Erweiterung „Macht des Goldes“ könnt ihr mehr Abwechslung in eure Anno 1800 Partien bringen. Diese Auftragskarten bieten euch neue Möglichkeiten, Gold zu verdienen und auszugeben.

—description from the publisher

Trading Places is a trivia game with a theme of traveling around the world attempting to complete business contracts. Money is awarded for this and the winner is the first player to accumulate a million pounds.

Movement is controlled by three dice which determine mode of travel (air, sea, etc.) and other details. While traveling players may answer "En Route" questions to continue. Entering cities requires answering more difficult "Visa Entry" questions and the "Contract Completion" questions are the most difficult.

Some travel spaces have limited capacity so players may have to answer "Challenge" questions to bump other players out.

The En Route and Challenge questions are general knowledge; the Visa Entry questions are more travel-oriented and the Contract Completion questions more business-oriented.

Eintracht Frankfurt Quiz
A combination of card and dice game containing 244 questions and 976 answers, in 6 difficulty levels.

Rules:
The youngest player begins. if he rolls a 2, the player to his left read out question 2 and the four possible answers on the back. If his answer is correct, he receives 2 victory points.
If he threw a 3, he would be asked the question 3. For the correct answer he would receive 3 victory points. And so on.

Each player only answers one question. 
Then it is the turn of the next player.

The winner is the first player to reach 50 points (or whatever victory conditions players agree upon)

Spot On requires a selection of cards from two standard decks: 3 black cards of each value from 3-9 and 12 red court cards. Players also each need a pawn and 12 counters in their color; one side of each counter needs to be marked with a spot.

The cards are shuffled and laid face-up in a 6x6 grid. On a turn a player places their pawn next to a row or column and rolls the die. They count that many cards from the pawn and place a counter on it, spot up, placing on top of an opposing counter if necessary. If necessary the count wraps around to the start of the row/column.

The player may then roll again and place another counter. This continues until they decide to stop or they roll a card which they already have a counter on (from this or a previous turn). In the latter case all the counters they placed this turn are removed. Otherwise they flip over their just-placed counters, remove any opposing counters from these cards, and take any of these cards that are courts. If they have counters on all three black cards of a given number they take those as well.

The game ends when all court cards have been taken. Each set of black cards is worth the cards' number. Each court is a point unless a player has seven or more, which is worth 12 points.

Now your expanding continent can include iconic landscape features! Add Kilimanjaro, the Sahara, Serengeti, and others to your developing world. And of course, there are more animals to increase the population of your ecosystem - from Nile Crocodiles to herds of Zebra. New Horizon introduces a new type of card mechanic to play these landscape features, and each interacts with the landscape in a unique way that will affect all players’ choices throughout the game. Can your plans withstand the creeping expansion of the Sahara or benefit from the deep jungle of the Congo Basin?

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Little Flower Shop: Basket – Game Boy Geek promo card
description:
Promotional basket card offered during the Game Boy Geek - Season 8 - 2020 Kickstarter campaign.

This new basket card allows you to gain $2 and draw a random card.

Subbuteo Shuffle Card Game


     Pass the ball from player to player by matching the balls on the cards, then shoot for goal! 


The first team to score three goals wins

     Includes 45 Player Cards, 6 Goalie Cards and 1 Coin Card 


Rules:
1. Pick up your four Players. 
2. Work out how many Players you can play in a horizontal line (your ‘pass line’) on the table in front of you. (You can only play a card if the ball on it matches the position(s) of the ball(s) on the card(s) next to it ) when two Player cards are played next to each other, they make a complete ball.. 
3. If you can play one, two or three Players to start your pass line, do that. If you’re able to play all four Players, only play three onto the table and skip to the Shoot for Goal! 
4. At the end of your turn, pick up new cards from your Players pile so that you’re holding four again. Play switches to the other team. Continue taking turns until you can play your fifth Player card into your pass line, It’s time to Shoot for Goal! 
You can play all four of your Players on your first turn, OR if you can play a fifth Player on a later turn, it’s time to Shoot for Goal. Choose which Player you’re going to use to shoot for a goal. 
The Opposing team: Look at your opponent’s pass line and work out where you think the ball might go next. Choose which of your Goalies you think will save the shot! 
Both teams: Play your chosen cards onto the pass line at the same time. 
Did your goalie save the ball? 

         Yes! Give the Goalie back to your opponent and remove your last two Players from the pass line. Put your two Players to the bottom of your pile. Play switches to the other team. 
         No! GOOOAAAL! Remove all cards that have been played by both teams and put them to the bottom of your Player piles.
    


Start playing again, with four new Players each, The team that let the goal in takes the first turn. 
Then there are special moves Tackling, Substitutions and Passing Back.

The winner of the game is first player to score 3 goals.

Distrix is an award-winning game of skill in which two players, Red and Blue, battle to control a 6x6 region called The Matrix.  The number in each square of the matrix equals power, and Red and Blue are equally powerful.  During the game, players use colored tokens to group the squares into 9 districts, one square at a time.  As districts are formed, power becomes unevenly allocated.  Red controls some districts, Blue controls others.  The game ends when one token occupies each square and there are no legal moves remaining.  Whoever controls more districts is the winner.

The idea is simple, but play is incredibly challenging.  To win, you must cleverly spread your power across many districts while concentrating your opponent's power into few districts.  This is how election districts are rigged, or gerrymandered, in the real world.  But, in Distrix, an opponent responds to your every move.  Each district is a tug-of-war that you want to win narrowly or lose by a lot.  But be careful...you may accidentally win it by too much or barely lose it!  Nine districts are in play, so there's a lot to keep your eye on.  Spatial awareness is critical because the shape of one district affects another.  And there's a special rule in the endgame--that allows squares to be reassigned from large districts to adjacent smaller ones--that can thwart even the best game plan, turning an expected victory into a stunning defeat.  Overall, it's a pure battle for power where basic arithmetic, spatial awareness, move timing, and move counting make all the difference.

A devilishly challenging affair, Distrix has the conceptual simplicity, aesthetic elegance, and strategic sophistication typical of classic games like chess and go.  But with real-world relevance, endgame surprises, razor-thin victory margins, and a different matrix every time, Distrix is a game of skill like no other.  Are you ready for the challenge?

Distrix won the Best New Abstract Game Judges Award at the 2021 UK Games Expo and a silver medal in the 2020 Serious Play Tabletop Game Competition.  It is for 2 players ages 14 and up and takes 30-60 minutes to play.  Each game set includes 36 tiles, 9 home base tokens, 99 expansion tokens, 9 scoring tokens, a scoreboard, rulebook, and rule summary sheet.

Distrix Edition 3.0 was released in October 2024 and is now available at DistrixGames.com.

—description from the publisher

A young and unknown composer presents to the manager of the Opera House his latest creation, the score of an opera that fascinates the manager, who tries to appropriate it. There is a struggle between the two and the manager throws a vial of acid at the composer that burns his face turning him into a monster. The composer then took refuge with his work in the cellars of the theater. There he starts a new life and builds his own world. Years pass and one day, captivated by the voice and beauty of a young Prano, he decides to kidnap her to perform his great opera for him.

The "detectives" must enter the gloomy cellars of the theater, discover the secret passages that lead to the phantom's lair and rescue the soprano, but they must be very careful not to be discovered by the phantom that would mark them with a deadly ink tampon.

Players can become these "detectives" and put an end to the misdeeds of the Phantom of the Opera. The first player to return to the entrance having managed to rescue the soprano wins the game.
family: Books: The Phantom of the Opera

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: Battlegrounds – Avengers vs. Masters of Evil
description:
Get Started with Battlegrounds!
The Avengers are a team of Earth’s Mightiest Heroes – can they defeat the Masters of Evil – a team of Earth’s most menacing villains? Each figure in HeroClix Battlegrounds comes with 2 cards to match their HeroClix dial; one is specifically geared toward learning to play, while the other is good for players who have already played a few games. Featuring fan-favorite heroes and recognizable villains, it will appeal to casual fans and Marvel veterans alike!

The Avengers feature Captain America, Black Widow, Iron Man, Hulk, and Captain Marvel. The Masters of Evil include Mach-X, Baron Zemo, Red Skull, Winter Soldier, and Ultron! Different combinations and teams will face off head-to-head in normal HeroClix combat or scenarios unique to this product!

This boxed-set is a great way for 2 players to learn HeroClix!

HeroClix Battlegrounds contains everything up to 4 people need to play HeroClix (Exact game components subject to change):

10 HeroClix Figures
20 HeroClix Character Cards
18 Object and Terrain Tokens
20 Action Tokens
4 Full-Color Maps
2 Six-Sided dice with the Avengers logo
1 HeroClix Powers & Abilities Card
1 HeroClix Core Rulebook
8 Scenario/Campaign Cards
6 Double-Sided Bystanders (for Scenario/Campaign use only)

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: セメタリーセメラレタリー (Cemetery Conqueror)
description:
セメタリーセメラレタリー (Cemetery Conqueror) is a game from Japan. You are the caretaker at an old cemetery. You hear the voices of the dead call out for their families to visit. But all the graves are dirty and overgrown. You set to restoring the graves, so the families will return again and honour their ancestors.

During the game you use your dice to make money, and then bid in auctions to complete work patterns using cubes, restoring the different graves in the cemetery and expanded your influence over the graveyard.
user summary

ゾンビデル (Zombidel), meaning 'Zombies & Zilches', is a trick-taking card game from Japan, set in a movie world of zombies. The theme draws heavily on the film Dawn Of The Dead, with the players trying to escape the shopping mall occupied by zombies.

By playing out tricks, you try to remove the weak and useless zombies from your hand, and avoid being killed by stronger zombies. If you can clear your hand of any shopping mall cards, you have got free. Now you need to escape by helicopter or motorbike.

But if you take too long, you might end up back in the shopping mall!

The game is not too serious, the graphic design is humourous instead of gory.
user summary

As of August 2021, we are remaking it with English rules and new illustrations. 
We will be using a kickstarter for it. It's primarily intended to help outside of Japan, so be interested!
publishers: ボボン・ボン・ボジワーイ連邦 (The Bobon Bon Bojiwaai Union)

description:
It's Contagious! is a pocket card game for fast, fun and strategic game-play. Your goal is to discard all your cards first and have the lowest viral load.

A climbing game (like Rummy and inspired by Escalation), cards are placed in a center pile increasing in count until a player can no longer best the previous amount (or use a Power Card).

Gameplay gets a boost of fun and strategy from a variety of Power Cards and additional rules keeps the game much more exciting.

GAMEPLAY

Players shuffle the deck. Each player is dealt 6 cards. The remaining cards are placed, face down, to create the center pile. The last person sick starts (or last round/game's loser) by playing a card face up to start that round's "Viral Load." Players always draw back up to 6 cards in their hand and play passes clockwise.

The next player then has to beat the current Viral Load's value (or match it using Matched Play). If they can't beat it with a higher card, or are able to utilize a Power Card, then they must take that Viral Load and add it to their own Viral Load. The next round starts with the player who played the last card (except when Contagion™ is played).

Play continues in this manner until all cards in the center pile are exhausted and the game ends when the first person plays their last card. All players then count the total number of cards in their Viral Loads and in their hand (if any are remaining).

-description from designer

Solo dice placement in a restaurant kitchen. Roll dice to determine your crew for the turn. Fulfil orders and manage kitchen events. Make it through your shift (order deck) while maintaining tidiness and morale and you win.

—description from the designer

The players have to move their 3 tokens from their 3 starting spaces to his destination space 7 in the center of the game board. Each player moves on his own track. Movement is determined by 3 dice. On certain results the players may move one ore more tokens and roll again. If a player rolls a result, that does mot allow him to move, the next player takes his turn. A space may be occupied by more than one token. On the roll of 1-1-1 or 1-2-4 (Jule) all 3 tokens are moved back to the starting spaces.

The first player to get all 3 tokens on space 7 wins the game.

Today is Chemistry class and we will have to get the… Perfect Formula!

We will play to create compounds with different liquids and densities (colors and numbers) in a fun way. You will have to think carefully about what type of card to choose at all times, but be careful… special cards can benefit or harm the formula by making it explode!

The objective of the game is to obtain the highest number of points at the end of the game. To get the points you have to build chemical formulas with the cards.

On the player's turn, a liquid or density card is drawn and added to the formula in each player's "test tube".

Well, find special cards that benefit when creating the combination or cards that will make your formula explode.

A fast and fun game for the whole family.

Playing, in addition to developing mental calculation and key competence in science and technology, we help enhance functions such as executive decision-making or working memory.

-description from publisher

Don't listen to what they say, farming is still fun! Grab some seedlings, hoe, and watering can and try to grow some vegetables during your first year in this hobby full of dirt, effort, and satisfaction!

A co-winner in design category of 1-card Print and Play Contest 2020 packs an action selection mechanics and a bit of dice-rolling right into this compact package.

The game runs through 3 seasons and each season consists out of 6 turns. Each turn roll a die, place in on available spaces and choose one of four actions available. Raise the vegetable dice, eliminate the dice representing costs of farming, and earn the most points.

Players try to beat their best score. On the back of the card  there is a scoring table the player can compare their achieved score with.

-description from designer

boardgame: Nantes: Un jeu de questions et d'humour sur Nantes et sa metropople
description:
Nantes: Un jeu de questions et d'humour sur Nantes et sa metropople is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.

Travel the board representing Nantes and its metropolis and answer questions and riddles.
Nearly 550 written questions and photos with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with themes such as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked Nantes and its metropolis.

"The greatest strategists seek to conquer the 5 kingdoms of the sun, for which they will increase the population of each kingdom, gather resources and spiritual energy, to dominate the 5 kingdoms of sun and become the greatest conquerors."

In Kingdoms of Sun we must dominate the largest number of kingdoms to reach the final score, but not only that, we must also obtain gems and develop one of our warehouses to the maximum to add the most points and become the ruler of Kingdoms of Sun. This Roll and Write gives a challenge when choosing which kingdoms we are going to try to conquer, we must be attentive to the actions of the other players who are also looking to conquer the 5 kingdoms.

On his turn, the player will roll the dice, choose one of the results that can serve him and proceed to mark this result on his game sheet, then he can make purchases with gems that he obtains to begin the conquest of one of the 5 chosen kingdoms. At certain times, you must acquire residents to collect more points.

The player who scores the most points in all the kingdoms and for the gems obtained throughout the game will win.

—description from the designer

Generals is a strategy game for 2 to 4 players that can be played 1v1 or 2v2. Each player is the commanding general of a faction and must use tactical and strategic resource management to conquer their opponents.

During the game each player has to organize military units on the map, build structures and recruit more units to attack the opponent and gain Victory points.

Four asymmetric factions each with special abilities and skill cards are fighting for success. Upgrades and special carda are available to all players making the game unpredictable and incredibly strategic.

—description from the designer

Arkaroll & Write is a roll and write game in which we will pretend to be the pilots of the Arkadya ship, our mission is to destroy the energy blocks that prevent the ship from entering the planet Arkus to leave supplies, at the same time we will score for the blocks of color destroyed, where the pilot who manages to collect the most points throughout the 4 missions will win.
Move from left to right, shoot and get the most points, but be careful you must manage the results of the dice well to be as efficient as possible.
Throughout the game we will evoke the experience of the mythical arcade game of the 80's ... what wonderful years.

In the game you must manage the throwing of the dice so that it is more efficient to be able to move and shoot the colored blocks, you will add points for the different destroyed colored blocks, you must optimize your dice since there are a number of rounds that must be completed before adding scores.

—description from the designer

Pen & Paper Baseball is baseball you can play in the comfort of your own home. Get some pencils, some paper and a couple dice, gather around the table with a friend or two, and play ball! Ballpark hotdogs and draught beer not included.

P&PB has all the rules you need to play a whole season of baseball - pitching, hitting, fielding and running, with rules for simulating long seasons without playing over 100 games and rules for team development. Several sample teams are included, or roll up your own.

And since baseball fans have been denied Opening Day this year ... P&PB is free until the baseball season begins ... even if takes until 2021.

—description from the publisher

2-4 player bluffing and dice rolling card game set in a science-fiction universe.

Each player starts the game with three Eye of Truth cards and ten hero cards.

Heroes have stats (strength, courage, wisdom, honor, and temper) on the back and stats (attack, defense) on the front. Heroes also have abilities ok the front. Players may never look at the backs of their cards.

The attacking player calls out one of the stats from the back of the cards, which could be truth or a lie. The defending player(s) call out the attacker's value for that same stat, which could be true or a lie. The attacking player announces the attack value on his/her card. The defending player(s) also do this regarding the defense value of their card. Finally, each player rolls a die and adds these three values (their die roll, the value they called out from the front, and the value that was called out to them from the back) and the highest value takes the trick and collects all cards played.

If a player believes that a called was false, they may play an Eye of Truth card.

If a player ever runs out of Eye of Truth cards or hero cards, they are out of the game.

Two players compete as they battle champions to earn medals. Medals are used to buy more champions. Once all champions have been purchased, the players sum the value of their champions to determine the winner.

Roll and Die is a roll and write game in which we will summon monsters to kill the humans invading our town. In each round we will use dice rolls to define how many humans appear in different areas of our town and also how many monsters, and from which areas, we can summon. At the end of six game rounds whoever leaves the fewest surviving humans will be the winner.

—description from the designer

Contarlos ("Count Them") is a print and play game based on a deck of UNO cards. The objective is to collect high valued cards by matching the color and using lower valued cards to add up to at least the high value card's number. After 4 rounds, count the total value of the large numbered cards. Whoever has the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Take to the seas Pirate Captains! The merchants are over burdened with goods and money and need your assistance to relieve them of their troubles. But be leery! The King's navy is on the lookout for the Jolly Roger. Get your loot, sell it, and be the one with the most wealth at the end of the season by any means necessary!

Scourge of the High Seas is played over 5 rounds where players will move their ship to attack merchants, islands and the navy in order to gain supplies to sell at market in trade for money. Once sold, bury the money and treasure to keep it safe for the end of the game. Whoever has the most points at the end of the game wins.

FEATURES

     3-6 players
     90-120 Minute Playtime
     200+ Pieces and Tokens
     Deck of treasure cards


GAMEPLAY
Three to six players can set sail on the ocean to try and gain the most money. Players will search out merchant ships or royal held islands and plunder them for supplies, treasure and money. With the supplies, the players can deliver them to nearby markets for selling to further increase their money. However, a captain's fortune is not safe until they can successfully bury it on the hidden island. Do not hold onto too much loot or another player may decide you are an easier way to grow their fortune.

Each player will have crew tokens to assign positions on their ship board in secret. When all players have made their assignments, everyone reveals their choices and whoever has the most speed will move first. Crew assignments may depend on many factors including wind direction, nearby targets to attack, other players and the navy ships out on patrol.

Each round the merchants and navy move around on trade routes, while the King's islands produce more supplies to be sold at market. Each market has its own demands for the four different types of supplies and it changes as player's sell their ill-gotten goods. In addition, every round has shifting winds making every decision in every round impactful.

Will you go for the island that has a bunch of one resource in hopes the market demand is high? Or will you risk confrontation with the navy to steal from the merchant ships? Perhaps that other pirate ship is too encumbered by its own fortunes and is ripe for the taking. The choice is yours and each game has a different starting layout giving unending variety.

—description from the designer

Purchase rooms to improve your dungeon, collect more and more resources to hire exquisite minions, and challenge daring heroes to grow your infamy in this competitive PVE game.

Each turn, the roll of dice determines what resources you receive. Rooms will make your rolls more lucrative, allowing you to purchase better rooms, hire better minions, and use better skills.

Lure in heroes, rolling dice to pit your minions against them. Once you defeat enough, you can lean on your renown to challenge elite heroes for victory points.

-description from publisher

boardgame: ボードゲーム工場～魔女と弟子たち～ (Boardgamemill ～Witches and Apprentice Fairies～)
description:
ボードゲーム工場～魔女と弟子たち～ (Board Game Koujou ～Majo to Deshi Tachi～) Boardgamemill ～Witches and Apprentice Fairies～ is a speed co-operative game produced by a Japanese company that actually manufactures boardgames for doujin circles.

In this game, the players are apprentice fairies working under craftspeople in the witches' board game mill! The number of Blueprint cards to complete and ship in 5 minutes will determine the players' level of craftsmanship.

Place the tiles on the Mill board according to the Blueprint cards, inspect and then ship them!

The tiles come in 4 shapes, 5 colors, and 3 different surface textures (glossy, embossed matte, and embossed grain), so try to identify them carefully. The craftspeople basically work with one hand!

First, choose a witch or senior fairy's magic to help your work. When you begin running out of the tiles, start on the fun supply mission to get them back.

職人の妖精の見習いになって、魔女のボードゲーム工場で働こう！
５分間に、設計書カードを何枚完成して出荷出来るかで、プレーヤーの職人レベルが決まるよ。
設計書カードの通りに工場ボードにタイルを並べて、検品したら出荷！
タイルには４つの形、５つの色、３種の表面加工（ツヤツヤ、絹目エンボス、石目エンボス）があるからしっかり見極めよう。
職人の作業は基本片手で行います！
作業を助ける魔女や先輩妖精の魔法を最初に選んでね。
使えるタイルが少なくなったら、楽しい仕入れミッションをして、タイルを取り戻します。
publisher's summary
publishers: Banjo Yugi Seisakujo BGM (盤上遊戯製作所 BGM)

description:
It is a baseball card game that anyone can easily enjoy.

- Various tactical cards reflecting baseball elements
- Easy game rules
Hits or home runs if the pitcher and batter numbers match
- Player stats
Game management using each player's stats

How to play
① They are divided into home team and away team.
② Select the first hit.
③ The attacking player mixes 16 batter cards and the defending player mixes 16 pitcher cards and arranges them as shown in the picture.
④ Each player draws 5 cards from the top of the deck and holds them invisible to the other side.

Game progress
① Each player turns one player card over and puts it in front.
② The player playing the batter card shouts “hit” or “home run”.
③ The player cards are turned over and compared.

When you shout “hit”
If there is a match between the batter card and the pitcher card being compared, it is a hit. (2 numbers match double, 3 numbers match triple) If there is no match, the batter is out.

When you shout “home run”
If the number in the circle of the batter card being compared and the number of the pitcher card match even one, it becomes a home run. If there are no matching numbers, the batter is out.

④ The used cards are stacked on the used card pile, and the number of cards used from the new card pile is taken from the top to continue playing while maintaining 5 cards.
⑤ At 3 outs, they change their karate and play with the same rules.
⑥ The player who attacked immediately before writes the points earned in the inning on the scoreboard.
⑦ If you run out of cards while playing, shuffle the cards you used and reuse them.
⑧ You can use only one tactical card. (Tactic cards used with player cards are turned over so that the opponent cannot see them and placed next to the player cards being compared.)
⑨ The player with the most points wins at the end of the set inning.

boardgame: Ahead in the Clouds: Character Pack
description: The Character Pack consists of 2 new Impacta Skies Industrialist character cards and 9 chipboard tokens for tracking. 8 of the tokens are double sided character tokens to track contracts, and the 9th token is to track the rounds/blimp.

boardgame: Tall Tales: A Game of Competitive Story-Writing
description:
Tall Tales is about writing little stories out for each other and coming up with a fairy tale together. It takes something that a lot of people find intimidating and lonely—creative writing—and tries to make it lighthearted, fun, and just a little competitive. (Maybe more than a little for some of us.)

In Tall Tales, your group will write an original story together. Players each write their own scenes and submit them to the group in the hope that their scene will be chosen for the group’s tale. A player wins by scoring the most points, which players score by having other players vote for their scenes and also by voting for the scenes that are popular with other players.

Tall Tales begins with a warm-up called the Quest Round in which players begin to imagine the world they're building together. After the Quest Round, the game plays out over three Scene Rounds. Players in the Scene Rounds each draft a scene for the tale and then anonymously vote on which of the scenes best meets the criteria for the round. The scene with the most votes becomes part of the tale in each Scene Round until your group has a final, three-part tale.

At the end of Round 3, players should count up their point tokens. The player with the most points wins the game. The winner of the game reads the completed tale aloud to the group, and all players live happily ever after.

-description from designer

Thalassophobia: A deathly fear of the ocean’s depths, and for good reason. You are a diver exploring an underwater grotto when your oxygen tank springs a leak. With only six minutes of air left in your tank, can you navigate this oceanic labyrinth and return to the surface in time?

SETUP
Gather all Blinks together into a single cluster. They should appear as murky, dark-blue waters. To begin, long-press a single Blink to turn it into a Diver. Now the clock is ticking, and the game has begun!

GAMEPLAY
When you begin a game, one of your Blinks will turn white, representing your current position on the board. Your air tank, represented by the number of lights on your Blink, has enough air to last for 6 minutes.

The player’s air tank counts down from 6 minutes to 1

Any Blink adjacent to your current position will be visible, showing either open water or entangling seaweed.

The player can see the Blinks immediately adjacent, but the rest are murky

Move to an adjacent open water Blink by clicking it. When you do so, new Blinks will be revealed, and the Blinks you are no longer adjacent to will begin to fade into murky darkness.

By moving their position, the player lights more of their surroundings

As you explore, you may see a glimmer of light either in open water or on some seaweed - move onto that space to ascend towards the surface.


Keep in mind that these chances are fleeting, and can only be spotted if a space is visible, so it’s best to explore as much and as quickly as possible. If you find yourself trapped, you can always rearrange your Blinks to find a way out.

WIN CONDITION
Your goal is to get all the way to the surface and avoid drowning, which requires you to ascend a total of 6 times. Make it all the way to the surface within 6 minutes, and you win the game!

—description from the publisher

Rocks Off is a trivia game about Rock and Metal music, spanning more than five decades, brought to life with the combined knowledge of the staff of the German webmagazine www.powermetal.de.

The game reduces trivia to the core: questions, answers, victory points. Each card contains three questions in three different levels of difficulty, sometimes plus a bonus question about song lyrics. The questions on a card always are about three different bands, musicians or events, so players being confronted with a question about a band they don’t like won’t be punished thrice.

In the two-player-variant, players get a four cards each. The first player asks the questions of his first card starting with the lowest difficulty. If answered correctly, the second question is also read and the same for the third question. The answering player is awarded victory point cards for each question answered correctly based on the difficulty level, then the opponent reads his card. The game continues until all eight cards have been played. The player with the most victory points after these eight to twenty-four question have been asked wins the game.

In the multiplayer variant each player gets one trivia card. Questions are read and tried to answer in turn, first the easiest level of difficulty, then the second and finally the third. Whoever answers a question correctly first receives a victory point card according to the difficulty level of the question. After all questions have been read and victory points have been awarded, the player with the most victory points is declared winner. In case of a tie, all song lyrics questions are asked providing a bonus round after which the winner is determined.

—description from the designer

A solitaire microgame about trying to escape a treacherous island filled with villainous pirates, tigers, and a raging volcano. The game starts with a small deck, and as the volcano consumes the island, the deck gets smaller and smaller. Press your luck. Keep the necessary cards, while trying to shed the worst. Find food and water to keep your health up. Build a raft or signal a merchant ship, and escape before time runs out.

—description from the designer
Three cards "Fear"(in French language) for the game "Feelinks" that brings another feeling to the game.

boardgame: Dans les cordes: Hyper cute card
description:
A promo card released for Valentine's day in 2020 for the boxing game "Dans les cordes".
There is French text on the card but the icons make it understandable.
The card has to be mixed with other cards to be played.

2 cards (one blue, one red) for Worldwide football.
These two additional action cards have to be mixed with the others to be played.

The text means : Take one action card in the hand of your opponent.

Published with the crowdfunding campaign of the first expansion but later made available.

boardgame: Worldwide Football: Sauvetage sur la ligne
description:
2 promotional cards released together during the crowdfunding campaign of the second expansion, but later made available together.

"Sauvetage sur la ligne" can be used on opponent shoots.

Instead of throwing one dice to stop the shoot, play "paper-rock-scissors" with your opponent.

If you win, the shoot is stopped. If not, your opponent scored the goal.

boardgame: Space Born Horrors: Five Parsecs Scenario 4
description:
Scenario 4 for Five Parsecs From Home 2nd edition offers up a new breed of creatures to fight in the depths of space, suspiciously silent colonies and abandoned outposts.

10 new encounters are included, which can be met randomly in a regular campaign or deliberately sought out. 
Each features a new creature type with description and nasty new abilities, offering a tough challenge for a battle-hardened party of characters.

Mechanics have also been included for possible additional rewards and even follow up missions as interested organizations pay you to go back in for them.

—description from the publisher

This booster pack contains 4 randomly selected game figures out of 60 in the DC HeroClix: Icons set.

There are two different box art variants with the same random contents.

—user summary

A Topsy-turvy, point wranglin' challenge! 
Players take turns tossing horseshoes to try and ring them onto the suspended pole and platforms. Score a SITTER, a RINGER, or best of all, a HANGER, all while the whole thing swings more and more with every toss! Do you have the skill to score the most points and win?

In 1884, under advice from her spiritual advisor, Sarah Winchester began work on a house in California. Starting with an eight room farmhouse, she continued adding and renovating until she died in 1922. She believed she was haunted by the spirits of those killed by Winchester-brand rifles and that by constantly renovating the house she could keep the spirits at bay...

In The Winchester House, you start with a small farmhouse and add tiles to build out your house. Matching colors can give bluffs or debuffs, special rooms will help you keep the ghosts contained. Your goal is to place all your tiles without letting any ghosts escape or having the terror meter cap out.

—description from the designer

Rivers & Rafts is an expansion for Endless Winter: Paleoamericans. It adds new modular tiles to the terrain map, including riverways and unique starting locations. It also introduces landmarks which tribes can control to gain powerful Eclipse phase benefits.

- 3x 3-hex river terrain tiles
- 2x river start/end tiles
- 4x starting location tiles
- 4x wooden rafts  
- 5x landmark pieces
- 5x landmark cards
- 15x tribe cards (warrior)
- 5x animal cards (sea cow)
- 1x sacred stone
- 1x rulebook

-description from publisher

Get ready to stack and attack! Play a fast-paced card game of sandwich action, or learn the deeper tactical mode to make your topping king of the sandwich.

In the Gas Station Sandwich mode, you compete to add your topping cards on the sandwich faster than anyone else. Toppings must match, but bread is a wildcard: anything can go on bread, and bread goes on anything, except bread doesn't go on bread. Bread on bread is not a sandwich.

To win the game here, you race your opponents through 4 hands of 4, playing a card each turn. But if you can overlap more than 2 symbols on the sandwich, madness can ensue. Overlap 3, and give an enemy a card! Overlap 4, and you chain another turn, to place another card.

For the Tactical Sandwich War, territory cubes are added and you can protect parts of the sandwich as your own as you fight for dominance, representing one topping. To win the game, be the first to reach 25 of your topping on the sandwich. On your turn, you play a card with the goals of covering opponents' toppings with bread, gathering guard cubes, blocking off portions of the sandwich for your own use, protecting your own toppings... or preparing to ruin someone else's day.

—description from the designer

Cave Paintings is an expansion for Endless Winter: Paleoamericans. Each player gets a dry-erase player board which they can connect dots and draw lines onto to unlock powerful one-time bonus actions, ongoing Eclipse phase benefits, and end-game scoring possibilities.

- 4x Dry-Erase Player Boards, with legs
- 4x Dry-Erase Markers with Eraser Caps
- 1x 5th Action Column Tile
- 1x Megalith tile
- 1x Terrain hex tile 
- 1x Glacier Token
- 1x Sacred Stone
- 1x Rest Token
- 5x Culture Cards 
- 15x Tribe Cards (Painter)
- 5x Animal Cards (Hagerman Horse)
- 1x Rulebook

-description from the publisher

Pays De Saint-Malo Le Jeu ! is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.

Travel the board representing Saint-Malo and its countryside and answer questions and riddles.
Nearly 750 written questions and 150 photos questions with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with themes such as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked Saint-Malo and its country.

Ancestors is an expansion for Endless Winter: Paleoamericans. It adds a large variety of new cards into the game, including alternate animals cards, interactive Culture cards, Chief special ability cards, and more...

-description from publisher

The Battle for the Pacific was only the beginning. 
Victory at Sea is the game of naval combat during the Second World War. 
Throughout 1939–45, the nations of the world duelled across the oceans across the globe, only to discover the fundamental nature of naval warfare changing in the face of rapidly developing technologies. 
Now you can play out these confrontations on the tabletop with entire fleets drawn from the Royal Navy, US Navy, Imperial Japanese Navy, German Kriegsmarine or any of the other nations featured in Victory at Sea.

From skirmishes involving single destroyers hunting down merchantmen to the clashing of massive battleships, from invasions of islands across the Pacific to mastering waves of dive bombers, Victory at Sea enables you to fight exciting battles that take place on the oceans of World War II.

This rulebook is the ultimate resource for Victory at Sea players. It contains:

     The complete rules for fighting naval battles, including the use of aircraft, submersibles and coastal defences.
     Detailed background notes on the progression of naval warfare through WWII.
     28 historic scenarios, covering every theatre over the span of the whole war.
     Exhaustive fleet lists for all the major belligerents, providing game statistics for hundreds of unique ships, submarines, aircraft and MTBs.

boardgame: Victory at Sea: Royal Navy Submarines & MTB sections
description:
Victory at Sea: Royal Navy Submarines & MTB sections

Submarines: Designed for use in North European and Mediterranean waters, the S-class was manoeuvrable with a noted ability to crash dive extremely quickly. Combined with a large salvo of torpedoes, this was a successful design of pre-war years that was soon updated and put back into production.

MTBs: The Fairmile A was designed from the outset to use prefabricated components that could be produced by small businesses such as furniture manufacturers, which would then be assembled at shipyards. Capable of 25 knots, it mounted a 3-pounder gun and a pair of .303 machine guns, as well as Depth Charge.

Designed with the form of a destroyer’s hull, the Fairmile B (like its predecessor, the Fairmile A) was intended primarily as a submarine-chaser, and so was fitted with Depth Charge. Manufactured in large numbers, the Fairmile B was also famously used on the raid on St. Nazaire.

Capable of 26 knots, the Fairmile C was a motor gun boat, mounting two 2-pounders and eight machine guns of various calibres. It was mainly used for close escort duties and some clandestine missions.

Nicknamed ‘Dog Boat,’ the Fairmile D was highly adaptable and could be fitted with a range of armament that meant it could act as both motor gun and torpedo boat. Some were used by the Royal Air Force for long range rescue of downed airmen.

Contents:
3 S-Class Submarines
4 Fairmile MTB Sections (A,B,C & D)
Ship Cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Walking Dead: Something to Fear – Shiva Promo Card
description:
Promo card originally available from the 2020 Game Boy Geek Kickstarter campaign.

Text says:
On your turn: You may destroy this card to move one wound to the mob OR to steal up to one Savior and one Walker from another player.

boardgame: Tournament at Camelot/Avalon: Mischief Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of the Game Boy Geek 2020 Kickstarter campaign.

Text says:
May be used at any time: Before a melee begins, switch seats with an opponent. All cards, powers, and health totals stay with their players.

Discard after use.

As a famed mercenary seeking to establish your medieval empire, the prophecy has been commoner's talk for a while now: “Of dragon scales gather up 10, and so become the ruler of men!” But your arrival in the desolate lands has presented two hardships. Firstly, dragons don’t give up their scales willingly. And second, you’re not the only one who’s heard the prophecy.

Game play begins with card drafting as players create a unique hand to battle dragons and each other. Play continues in a series of rounds until 1 player wins by ending a round with 10 dragon scales or being the only player with at least 1 health token remaining.

Dragon scales are gained by contributing strength to defeat a dragon, looting from the dragon, or stealing from other players (if you kill another player you gain all of their dragon scales).

Each round has 3 phases.
Phase 1: (every round)
Players simultaneously play cards from their hand face-down. All cards are then revealed and resolved in a predetermined hierarchy (cards of the same hierarchy resolve clockwise from the dragon). 
Phase 2: (triggered only by specific phase 1 cards)
A repeat of phase 1, however only players who have used specific cards during phase 1 can participate.
Phase 3: (every round)
Dragon combat and loot distribution. Players combine their strength in an attempt to defeat the dragon. The dragon player draws the top 3 cards from the dragon deck selecting one to play against all other players. The dragon card increases or decreases the dragon's base strength, often adding targeted abilities*. If the dragon is defeated, all contributing players take dragon scales. If undefeated, all players take 1 damage from the dragon. Looters acquire loot (dragon scales or items) from the dragon irrespective of the outcome.

All non-core cards are discarded before and he role of dragon then shifts one player to the left (their mercenary becomes untargetable for the following round). A new round will then begin, rounds continue in this manner until a winner is determined.


    In a round with 2 or 3 players, the dragon becomes automated. In this case the top card of the dragon deck is chosen and targeted abilities affect all players.


Unique game elements include a mix of card-drafting, simultaneous-play, semi-cooperative and competitive tactics, and playing as both the dragon and dragon attacker (in different rounds).

—description from the designer

Mission Emission is a fun and fast, competitive, trick-taking and hand management card game for kids and adults, produced by Nightingame. This game challenges you on a mission to save India's states from global warming!

Each card of Mission Emission represents a state or union territory of India. These cards have 6 environment related stats (called Missions) like Forest cover, Air Quality, Population Density, etc.


     The active player chooses a Mission/category and plays a card. 
     After each player has played a card from their hand in turn order, the player whose card has the highest stat of the chosen Mission wins a Point Card of that Mission.
     Point Cards have victory points mentioned on them, and the player with the most points wins at the end of the game.


The fun lies in the Point Cards—the Point Cards opened later in the game are worth more points, so you may choose to save your best cards for later rounds. But be careful, the Point Cards may run out faster than you think! To add to the fun, some Point Cards are worth negative points, making this a competitive game which will leave you planning, plotting, sacrificing cards, and biting your nails right till the end!

The game rulebook also comes with 2 more alternative sets of rules, and many more on the official website www.nightingame.com, so you can play a new variation every time!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
クロスウォーズ (CROSS WARS) is a competitive game based on the crossword puzzle. The players each have the cards with the answer words for their opponents.

On your turn, choose to:
- ask to reveal the letter in a square,
- ask for a clue to guess a certain word, or
- announce the word you have guessed.

If you guess incorrectly, you cannot fill the squares. On the other hand, if you guess correctly, you can take an additional turn. The first player who fills all the squares on their sheet wins.

クロスワードパズルを元にした対戦ゲームです。
答えとなるクロスワードが決まっており、お互いに相手の答えとなるクロスワードが書かれたカードを持ちます。
手番では、特定の１マスの文字を教えてもらうか、特定のワードのヒント（キー）を教えてもらうか、
特定のワードを答えるかの３種類の行動ができます。
特定のワードを答えた場合、間違っていたらマスを埋めることはできませんが、合っていた場合はもう一度手番を行えます。
クロス面をすべて埋めたプレイヤーの勝利です。
publisher's summary

This game combines two previously separate expansions to Hostage Negotiator (Abductor Pack 5 and Abductor Pack 6).

It includes 32 cards.

This item combines two previously separate expansions to Hostage Negotiator (Abductor Pack 7 and Abductor Pack 8).

It includes 33 cards.

Mini-expansion promo for Tutankhamun. This pack features 5 new tiles that can be added to the base game. These tiles include 1 new artifact tile and 2 new gods, each with 2 tiles.

Included in the pack are 6 reference cards that explain the abilities.

Falcon Pectoral artifact tile - This is a single artifact tile that immediately scores two points. Deposit it in the tomb as usual when scored.

Set God Idol tiles (2) - Move up to 2 Artifact tiles from the Underworld to the end of the Nile next to the Tomb, extending the river.

Sekhmet God Idol tiles (2) - Swap an Artifact tile in your collection for any other Artifact tile in the Nile that has an equal or lesser point value printed on it.
