—description from the publisher

Nanodaption of Ahead in the Clouds.

If you haven't already noticed, Nat has been making tiny versions of our already tiny games. Now he takes on Ahead in the Clouds with just one card (plus rules and tokens). This is resource management in a very simple, elegant form.

—description from the publisher


Based on Ahead in the Clouds by Daniel Newman.

Use your limited actions to condense and convert the four basic resources, shared between both players. Bank an action for later or flip the card with a Cloudburst to bring different actions and contracts into play. Time it right to send them out and fill contracts. The first player to fill 3 contracts wins.


Released in the March 2017 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

Nanodaption of Fever Chill.

Way back at the end of 2015, we released a little wallet game called Fever Chill. It's nearly sold out at this point, but revived by Nat Levan in his line of Nanodaptions that capture the essence of the game in a smaller package. In Chill Pill, you will simultaneously choose actions to try to raise the infection level above 7 without raising the temperature too high.

—description from the publisher


Based on Fever Chill by Kenneth Thompson.

Now you play the viruses competing to infect a body. Players simultaneously infect a part of the body to increase their infection level, but too many viruses in one place also raises the temperature. The doctors always target the virus with the highest temperature, so you can only win by infecting faster than the doctors can treat or outlasting your fellow viruses.

This game is back to basics, with two board variations on one two-sided card and a rule card. Just add 2 tokens per player (and an optional die) to play anywhere from 2-4, 5, 6, or more.


Released in the July 2017 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

To survive the long and terrible winter approaching, the dwarves need to prepare. It’s time to collect gems, build castles and stock up enough food. The fire dragons are sleeping. The giants of winter, lurking. The fall is precious and is vital to plan your actions wisely.

Dwar7s Fall is a game about collecting gems in order to trade it for food. In the meantime, you also will need to build your dwarf kingdom and slay some monsters. In this game you have multiple paths to winning by carefully choosing your actions.

Players take turns to perform their actions. Each turn you can do 3 actions (place a realm tile, place one dwarf in a tile, or move one dwarf by one tile).

Each tile has a pre-requisite of how many dwarfs of one player are needed to perform one kind of action. For example: emerald mines have always three slots, but you will need only two dwarfs to take one emerald. When this task is done (collect one emerald) your two dwarfs will return to your pool.

When a player completes three goals (both open and secret goals are taken into account), the endgame begins. Players who had not yet played that round make their last turn and then the game ends. The points are calculated and the winner is whoever has more points.


Note:  This edition also contains the special class meeples from Dwar7s Fall: Royal Decrees; the Guardian, the Warrior, and the King. It does not include the Scenario or Royal Decree cards.

boardgame: Underwater Cities: Cooper & Flexible Friend Promo
description:
A set of two personal assistant cards, for use with Underwater Cities - New Discoveries:

Cooper - the contract specialist dog: Draw 3 of the discarded Government Contracts. Choose 1 of them and put the other 2 back in the box. Place the card in your playing area. Only you can complete that contract, at any time, during your turn. After completing it, return it to the box.

Flexible Friend - You can always play green cards as if they match the color of any chosen action slot.

Whose Treasure is a card game for people to make the time more enjoyable with friends. Players try to make full completed maps as soon as possible in the FIRST MODE match or as many as possible in the FINAL MODE match. Players take turns to put their cards on the table and draw cards from the deck to keep 4 cards in their hands.

Since many maps are almost complete over time, the game becomes more exciting, so players try to prevent others from completing the maps.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling: Eye Karamba
description:
A new character, Eye Karamba, for SuperP.L.E.X.!

If you haven't played SuperPLEX, it's our 2016 nod to M.U.S.C.L.E. style wrestling. Over the top characters that are slightly out-of-this-world, fighting with extra special unique moves. The game came with 2 characters and we've released a few since (to change it up, or to play as tag teams). Eye Karamba is a massive 4 armed beast that feels like the characters I loved as a kid in the 80's and 90's.

—description from the publisher


Released in the April 2017 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.
family: Game: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling

boardgame: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling: Tag Team Mode Bonus Pack
description:
Six additional cards for SuperP.L.E.X. to play longer or 4P games. With one base game and a tag team bonus pack, 2 players can play an intense head-to-head Tag Team game. If players have two sets of the game then they can use the bonus pack to play up to 4 players.

Includes 2 new characters for Tag Team action:

     Volcanessa
     C'Food Lou


The bonus pack also includes their finishing moves and 2 tag cards to switch wrestlers.
family: Game: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling

boardgame: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling: Nerdman
description:
A 2-card set promo featuring a new character, Nerdman.

Nerdman is based on designer Ryan Cowler's actual wrestling character, P.O.N.G. Ryan is part of Oakland, CA's Hoodslam league.
family: Game: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling

boardgame: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling: Cage Match Ring Playmat
description:
A huge playmat that will make your table presence 100% more awesome.

The playmat also includes a unique Cage Match Ring that deals damage to characters that are pushed beyond the turnbuckle spaces.
family: Game: Super Pocket League Extreme Wrestling

description:
Iron and Fire is a moderate level complexity game covering the battles fought by the Peruvian Ironclad Huáscar during the 1879 War of the Pacific against Chile. It outclassed everything it met but there was only one (think of the SF classic, Ogre, but on the waters this time).

The Peruvian navy owns an ironclad monitor that outclasses everything else afloat in South America.  But can it win a war by itself?  Look at the 1879 "War of the Pacific", between Chile and Peru, which displayed naval technology one notch above the American Civil War.

-description from publisher's website

First Strike looks at, well, the "first strike" by British airplanes. On Christmas Day, 1914, float planes were brought near land by the earliest "seaplane tenders" and turned loose on an "air reconnaissance" by planes which just happened to be carrying bombs and looking for Zeppelin sheds. There was also hope of luring out the German fleet, and an unexpected result of having the British naval force attacked by German airplanes.

Who would guess the very first "carrier-based air attack" would happen on Christmas Day, 1914? Yes, it happened! British seaplane tenders supported a "reconnaissance" against the German naval base at Cuxhaven . . . carrying bombs in the hopes of destroying the Zeppelin sheds that were there also.  The fleet was attacked by German seaplanes in turn, and a remarkable adventure ended with few casualties on either side. It would make a great movie, but for now, it's a great game!

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Under Ten Flags: Hunting the Raider Atlantis 1939-1941
description:
Under Ten Flags starts early in 1939 when the German merchant raider Atlantis made its way into the South Atlantic to sink and capture Allied cargo vessels. Its crew used their skill in disguising the appearance of the vessel to aid its encounters or avoid enemy warships. The journey of the raider Atlantis lasted for over 600 days and traveled over 100,000 miles.

Follows the 100,000 mile journey of the German "auxiliary cruiser" Atlantis, a "merchant raider" capable of changing its shaped and configuration as well as its flags.  Atlantis is probably the most successful merchant raider of all times when combining total tonnage sunk and how long it forced the Royal Navy to chase it; plus, it captured documents of strategic importance that actually impacted the war.  The "adventure" sounds like something from a movie, and in fact, did inspire one from which our title comes.  Each player is challenged to "do better" than their historical counterparts.

-description from publisher's website

A new character for Wild Cats, Hitcat, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club January 2016 package.

January 2016 games were inspired by Pulp Fiction. This should add a little cult film twist to Wild Cats.

You can add the Hitcat to your game for a little taste of being a secret hitman. You want to assess the group and vote with the crowd to tie for the win. If you mess up, you'll make multiple other players win, which is never good.


RULES
You MAY lie. 
The player you vote for is eliminated. This may cause multiple eliminations and multiple winners. You tie for the win if only one player is eliminated.

XXI, a solitaire game set in 1943 that challenges the player (acting as both Speer and Dönitz) to organize and complete construction of a fleet (or at least “enough”) of the new German Type XXI submarine. However this new sub is far larger, more complex and more expensive, and requires much more manpower and scarce resources than previous submarines. Just a few of these new Type XXIs will do nothing. Only a radical transformation in shipbuilding techniques to mass produce the design - and not giving in to setbacks - will enable this “wonder weapon” to arrive in time....

The advanced Type XXI U-boat has the technology needed to survive and thrive in the Allied-controlled seas, and is a potential game-changer to the war's outcome . . . if it can be supplied in sufficient numbers in time.  (In reality, only two went to war.) This is your challenge as a player, operating functionally as both the Naval Commander-in-Chief and Armaments Minister, working to foster production and work around wartime setbacks.

-description from the publisher's website

A new character for Wild Cats, The Dude Cat, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club February 2016 package.

It breaks the rules of the game a little, but is thematic perfection. It's also a fun twist on the game when you can mess with people without any worries of messing up yourself.

Here we decided to go with Dude Cat, the cat that has alredy won when the game starts. He doesn't have a worry because he knows that it'll all work out in the end.


RULES
You must LIE. 
You're the Dude Cat, so you've already won.
Have some fun with it.

A new character for Wild Cats, Scissorpaws, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club March 2016 package.

Edward Scissorhands. This movie is extremely iconic due to the main character and the clashing 1950's household perfect that he is placed in. It's one of Tim Burton's most well known (and one of 2 Tim Burton films that made 2016's list).

Scissorpaws is a new Wild Cat that votes with 2 fingers (like scissors!). They can point at 1 player or 2. He is trying to eliminate Bad Cat, but could fuel a Wild Cat win if he presses his luck too far.


RULES
You must LIE. 
You vote with two fingers. You can point to multiple players or to one player twice. Votes for you do not count.
You win if Bad Cat is eliminated.

An Oceans 11 inspired Wild Cats card, the Cat Pack, that allows a player to look at and redistribute the roles as they'd like, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club April 2016 package.

This new character stops the game right after it starts, takes all of the cards and distributes them as he/she pleases. Then picks a role to align with. It's fun heady stuff for a fun little headgame.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
IMMEDIATELY REVEAL YOURSELF.
Gather all roles, look, & redistribute as you like. Declare a partner role without revealing the player. You tie for the win if your partner wins.

A Wonka inspired Wild Cats card, the Loompa Cat that forces a player to rhyme and get rid of any bad eggs, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club May 2016 package.

In the spirit of the film, with the Loompa Cat you need to ask questions in rhymes and you want the bad egg, I mean cat, out of the game. If you are too obvious, the bad cat might turn things around on you and start to rhyme as well. Gotta watch those bad cats.


RULES
You must tell the TRUTH.
The last word of all of your questions must rhyme.
You tie for the win if Bad Cat is eliminated.
2-4 Bus Drivers go head to head in a take-that, pattern-matching, strategy card game about public transportation. Get the most paying customers on and off your bus before the end of the line!

boardgame: Cow, Tiger, Santa Claus: Alternate Play Rules – Co-op
description:
A Board Game of the Month Club promo for January 2017 was a new Cooperative Alternate Play Rules set for Cow, Tiger, Santa Claus.

While it's winter-ish here in New Jersey, we still have plenty of long car rides. And instead of everyone fighting, we're going to work together and try to find some tough items (unless you pull the Santa Claus card in the next two weeks, that is).

This new rules card lets players join together and look for items as a group. More eyes should mean more points, but every 15 minutes you need to remove a card from play. When you find an item, it is scored and can not be scored again. As a reward, you may take a card that was removed from play back, and restart your 15 minute timer.


We're hitting the road again in Cow, Tiger, Santa Claus with the Co-Op rules card. This was a planned stretch goal that didn't make it. Instead of competing to be the first to find all three items, you are working as a group to get as many as possible before the clock resets your items.

—description from the publisher

The ultimate game of chance

A baseball fan’s game of choice

Game ncludes: Gameboard, player pieces, 1 set of die and score sheets (sample game included) with instructions

Ages 10 to adult for 1, 2 or more players

A new character for Wild Cats, Alley Cat, a charity card with all proceeds going to the Animal Welfare Association.


RULES
You must tell the TRUTH and LIE.
You win if you are eliminated.

(From the publisher’s Discord, regarding how to tell the truth and lie: “Over the course of the game you must do both“)

Nautilùs illustrates an old sailor game from the 18th century, which was popular to play amongst sailors during the crossings of the pacific. The game combines old myths and yarns of the sea.

The goal of Nautilùs is to bring 4 sailors, illustrated by marbles, from the vortex in 60 steps to the safe harbor. Inspired by the game concepts from "Mensch Ärger dich nicht", "Tac" and "Dog", the game is being played in teams(2 against 2). During the ride, the sailors have to face several threats and challenges such as wrong compasses, pirate trips and the legendary white whale Moby Dick. Moby Dick is powerful and merciless - protect yourself from him in a cave in order to reach the save Harbor!

No matter if it´s storm or sunshine - Nautilùs is a must on your next navy!

—description from the designer

A new character for Wild Cats, Office Cat, inspired in Office Space, and delivered in Board Game of the Month Club June 2016 package.

What is better that quitting your terrible office job? Well on this card, it's sticking it out and getting fired. The unemployment kicks in and is technically better. But Office Cat allows you to stick it to the main and quit mid-game. If you do, you are a winner, and the game continues. If you stick around until the end, you can be the only winner.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or Lie. 
You can quit. If you do, you tie for the win. If you stay and are eliminated, you are the only winner.

A new character for Wild Cats, Horror Cat, inspired in Dr. Frank-N-Furter from The Rocky Horror Picture Show, and delivered in Board Game of the Month Club July 2016 package.

Sticking with audience participation, Horror Cat involves a non-player. Yes, you need to find someone who is not in the game and get them to vote along with you to tie for the win. You can tell them "at the count of three, point to this person" and they may or may not go along with it. If it's the non-players at my game nights, they definitely will not go along with it. So there is an extra level to reading them, just like trying to read other players. At least with other players, the cards guide their intentions. Here, it's wide open.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or Lie.
Before the vote, reveal yourself and ask a non-player to also vote. If you both vote the same, you tie for the win.

A new character for Wild Cats, Baseball Furry, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club August 2016 package.

The Baseball Furry (a pun that was unavoidable), is all about voting with the group. No single player can be a gang on their own, so you need to watch for cues and follow along. OR better yet, you can lead the group. Both are completely valid and depend on your play style or surroundings.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
If you vote alone, your vote doesn't count. If you vote with majority, you tie for the win.

A promo for Silver from Bezier Games. This was available as a part of the Game Boy Geek Season 8 Kickstarter campaign.

The card has a value of 3 and says, "When faceup: Your faceup cards are protected from opponents."

If you have a Prince faceup in your village, other players may not remove, flip, or modify any of your faceup village cards in any way.

Other players may give you additional faceup cards, as long as they are not required to switch for an existing faceup card in your village.

-description from publisher

A new character for Wild Cats, Tabby Wiseau, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club September 2016 package, inspired on the cult film The Room.

This one is likely our deepest cut in the Cult Film Wild Cats series. But Tabby Wiseau just hates himself. He can vote himself out and actually eek out a win if others join along. You could really deep down try to imitate the most obvious target or take a meta route and just annoy people to see if they vote for you.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
You must vote for yourself.
You win if somebody else also votes for you.

A new character for Wild Cats, Rabbit Cat, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club October 2016 package, inspired on the cult film Donnie Darko.

Sticking with the theme, but bringing in the tangential universe, we have a Rabbit/Cat that makes players take back their votes and vote again.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
You do not vote. Reveal yourself after the vote. Count to three and players vote again. If the eliminated player changes, you tie for the win.

A new character for Wild Cats, Drummer Cat, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club November 2016 package, inspired on the cult film This Is Spinal Tap.

We wanted to have fun and feel the effects of spontaneous combustion. So of you lost last round, lose again.


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
If you were eliminited last game, you are immediately eliminated.
You tie for the win if you are not eliminated.

A new character for Wild Cats, Lydia Catz, delivered in Board Game of the Month Club December 2016 package, inspired on the cult film Beetlejuice.

Of course this is a card about speaking 3 times. With Beetlejuice as the main theme, we had to go there. It's required. Also a fun sneaky card!


RULES
You may tell the TRUTH or LIE.
If you ask the same question three times without anyone identifying you, the game ends immediately and you win.
Otherwise, you do not vote.

First Infinity Defiance expansion!

The characters of the Defiance, on who’s shoulders rest the fate of the Human Sphere. Named heroes even before they jumped to the far side of the ​Acheron ​wormhole. Are now considered outlawed.

Marked as infected, and compromised by the insidious Shasvastii and their dangerous Specular Killers. However, even when branded as traitors the crew of the Defiance will do their best to survive.

With the help of Ensign Katherine Cho of O-12, who does not believe the official propaganda of her superiors. Also with mercenary operator Raoul Spector, who has previously worked with Katherine while undercover.

—description from the publisher

Wonky Words is a Scrabble-like word game where you form words inside polyomino word holders before placing them.

Grab a shape, insert letters, play your word(s).

No individual turns. Simultaneous game play for fast action.

—description from the publisher

Years after returning from the other side of the Wormhole of Acheron and cleaning out their files, the crew members of the Defiance and a motley crew of collaborators – including Kendriat, an impetuous Morat, and Jaan Staar, a Tohaa with unsettling abilities - serve as freelance agents and soldiers of fortune under the banner of their own mercenary company: The Revenant Mobile Intervention Force.

The MIF owes its name to the Revenant, the current moniker for the old and famous space daring vessel - The Defiance, which was the first human ship to ever reach Combined Army space and return to tell the tale.

Aristotle’s Elements and Space is a Greek themed, interactive card game that is fun for the whole family and sure to keep you on your toes.  Lay down your cards featuring Ancient Greek philosophers, mathematicians, and gods and compete with your friends to see which element will triumph.  Best for 3-5 players ages 10+, it only takes 10 minutes to learn and 15 minutes to play.  Choose your cards carefully, call an element of surprise when you’re feeling lucky, and watch out that no one steals your tokens: Open up a box of fun and battle to reign victorious over the table.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Wild Tile Variant Kit for the Game of ACQUIRE
description:
The ACQUIRE Wild Tile Variant Kit is made to fit most editions of ACQUIRE games. This kit allows each player to start with a blank tile that can be played on any open square during their turn at any time during the game.

The ACQUIRE Wild Tile Kit includes six (6) blank tiles and an instruction booklet. Tiles are from actual ACQUIRE games that have been painted to hide the designations. The instruction booklet is printed on yellow card stock for maximum durability and readability.

The Wild Tile Variant allows each player to start the game with that one "key" tile that they may need at one point in the game. It increases the ability to create mergers and helps to alleviate frustration when a player has nothing but "bad" tiles and is unable to maintain a foothold in the game. Some games, with the luck of the draw, a player may never need their Wild Tile.

Included with this Wild Tile Kit are Lloyd's Rules of ACQUIRE, which were designed to help eliminate the one-sided games that can occur during the regular game play in ACQUIRE.

(The Wild Tile Variant is an idea conceived by Allan Wright)

—description from the designer

An additional character for Banned Books, from The Catcher in the Rye by J. D. Salinger, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club January 2018 package.

Holden:
(1) Curses: Advance any Progress Token one space and stall that Power.
(3) Foils: Bump all Progress and Power Tokens on one card to the end of their line.

An additional character for Banned Books, from The Color Purple by Alice Walker, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club March 2018 package.

Celie:
(1) Independence: Move any Power token back 1 space.
(2) Rebirth: If at only 1 Stamina, reset to starting Stamina (*).

An additional character for Banned Books,  from Go Ask Alice by Beatrice Sparks, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club April 2018 package.

Alice:
(2) Express: Reorder the action cards as you would like.
(3) Escape: Eliminate a Power card from the game. (once per game)

An additional character for Banned Books, from The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn by Mark Twain, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club May 2018 package.

Huck:
(1) Accept: Roll a die and move your Stamina to that number.
(3) Deceive: Reverse the lanes to be ordered as 3, 2, 1.

An additional character for Banned Books, from The Chocolate War by Robert Cormier, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club June 2018 package.

Jerry:
(1) Refuse: Do not flip actions face-down this turn.
(2) Showdown: Roll a die, if the result matches a Power's track, reduce the Power to 1. (can impact multiple Powers)

An additional character for Banned Books, from Animal Farm by George Orwell, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Club July 2018 package.

Old Major:
(1) Inspire: Shuffle and re-deal the action cards.
(2) Revolt: Skip the Powers this turn.

Will your zines be the best ones on the shelf? Will you be caught stealing copies?

In Zine Game, players earn points based on supplies, durable bindings, and other issues that zine publishers struggle with. Will you have the next great idea — or will your rivals destroy your hard work before it ever sees the light of day?

An additional character for Banned Books, from And Tango Makes Three by Justin Richardson & Peter Parnell, delivered in the Board Game of the Month Clu December 2018 package.

Tango:
(3) Warmth: Use any or all of the face up action cards in any order that you would like. Do not flip or re-order them after using them.

Who is the best cook on board? Will you be faster than your colleagues in providing the crew with tasty food? All without wasting precious ingredients?

Card game with Set collection.

Crazy climbing action game.
Wanna get funky?
Do the monkey!
Race your monkey up the tree!
Dump all your marbles into the trunk to win!

Every player starts with 5 coconuts, the first player to run out of coconuts wins. Use the ropes to make your monkey climb the palm tree and pour a coconut on top of the palm tree, the cononut will fall into a player tray at ramdom.

5 extra Tenants to play High Rise with! This will bring the number of Tenants in your game from 45 to 50.

The extra tenants are Demolition Company, Entirely Legitimate Business, Stockbroker, Law Firm and Marketing Firm w/ yellow letters EXP in the upper right corner.

Your newest furry Munchkin friends are back for the holidays! With festive art by Katie Cook, Munchkin Tails of the Season is the purrfect addition to Munchkin Tails (or any other Munchkin game that needs a dose of Christmas magic)!

—description from the publisher

School is in session!

Either the dungeon has flooded or you took a very wrong turn somewhere because you don't remember The Flaming Hellscape being this damp. No time to look back, however, as there are all sorts of fun new monsters to fight and odd new weapons to do it with. Are you the king of the sea, or will you take a dive? There's only one way to find out — check out Munchkin Something Fishy!

—description from the publisher

This game consists of collecting animals that belong to the same family and accumulate the most points according to the way they are played.

The deck consists in 38 cards (36 native animals and 2 rules cards).

Each card includes the name of the species, the family it belongs, the number of members of that family and the points it's worth.

How to collect points? There are 3 ways:
1. Reuniting an incomplete family, you obtain the sum of the points of the cards played.
2. Reuniting a complete family on the board, on different turns, you obtain the sum of the points of the cards + points indicated on the bonus card.
3. Reuniting a complete family in one turn, you obtain double points (from played cards + bonus)

At the start of the game each player has 5 cards, on each turn there are two phases:
1. Draw a card from the deck or discard pile and play a family (complete or incomplete).
2a. Say it's the end of the turn turn.
2b. Or the player can discard a card from the hand, then asking to opponent if he has a card that can be used to complete a family: if he does, he must give it to the player; if he doesn't, the player loses the discarded card.

At the start of the next turn, each player starts draw to complete a hand of 5 cards and start again until the deck is depleted.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Smart hens lay eggs and fight for their seats.
The way to get a place is math!
But you need to remember where the eggs are. You can remove it the moment you are off guard.
Who is the smartest hen?

<Purpose of the game>
1. You need to figure out the location and number of eggs to score more points than other players.

<Game ready>
1. Each player takes 20 pieces of the same color.
2. Randomly cover 25 lid tiles on the board.
3. Each player shuffles the cards and draws 4 cards.
4. The remaining cards are placed in a pile next to the board.

<Game play>
1. When the first player's turn comes, he uses it to add the required number of 4 cards in his hand.
ex) When using 2 cards: (1) card + (2) card = 3
ex) When using 3 cards: (1) card + (2) card + (3) card = 6
2. Open the divider cover, insert 1 game piece (egg), and then close the cover again.
3. Discard the used cards and draw cards from the pile for the number of cards used. This way the turn is over and the next person's turn begins.

<Game ends>
The game ends when all players' game pieces are used up. And when you open the lid, the one with the most eggs wins. Completing 3 and 4 items straight and diagonally gives 3 and 4 additional points each, and if there are discarded game pieces (eggs), the player with the most points by calculating -1 per piece wins.

Only one pirate can have the honor of the Pirate King. Unfortunately, the Pirate King's seat is empty, but you need to pay attention to other pirates to get that place. Can you be the first pirate king?

This game is for 2-4 players. Your goal is to earn more honor than anyone else and become a pirate king.

There are times when someone will take a risk to gain fame, and some will predict their own victory through reasoning. But be careful. Pirates always endure sacrifices.

A card that you think is a good card can be a bad card. Conversely, bad cards can give off a light that you thought was impossible.

Everything is in the hands of the Pirate King candidates.

<Purpose of the game>
1. The one with the most Corsair fame by summoning pirates wins.

<Game ready>
1. Distribute cards according to the number of players.
ex) 2 people: 8 sheets, 3 people: 6 sheets, 4 people: 4 sheets

<Game play>
1. Select a line, point to the player playing the game on the line, and draw one of those cards.
2. Then place one of the cards in the center (sea). This is the end of the turn.

    Players with 1 card cannot be selected.
    When placing in the center (sea), it cannot be deployed if there are hostile pirates.


3. All players play until one card remains.
4. All players reveal the cards in their hand if they have played until one card is left. The player who reveals the lowest numbered card wins. Players who win this way receive 1 fame chip.

    If (12) and (1) or (2) are played together, the player holding the (12) card wins.
    If (0) and (1) or (12) are played together, the player holding the (0) card wins.


<Game ends>
1. The player with 2 fame chips wins.

Di-Li-Do is an IDEAL GAME TO PLAY ALONE. After your eyes are tired from any computer / mobilephone / TV / smartphone etc - screen - it is great to spend time playing  just with a piece of paper, pencil and 2 (d6) dice. Also you can organize a tournament for any amount of players and for any amount of time. To start you need to download a score sheet and it is the score sheet for one player.

Goal of the game: To get as much as possible points.
To get points you must make as many continuous Lines as possible with Dots along the vertical, horizontally and diagonally. The more Dots in the line, the more points you get. You must have at least 3 Dots in a Line to get any points. First it looks like a very simple game but more you play more hints and tactics you will find.

ROLL DICE and you get two numbers from two ordinary 6-sided dice. The numbers on the Dice are the coordinates where you need to put a Dot on a gaming board.
If the coordinates are occupied and the player cannot place a Dot, then he receives a penalty point (draw a cross in the low left of the gaming board). Each GAME lasts up to 3 penalty points. After getting the third penalty point - GAME ends – start to count the lines.

​

—description from the designer

DiCroNo is a variation of Tic-Tac-Toe / Noughts and crosses / Xs and Os, and to play, you need two d6 normal dice, two pens/pencils, and a print-and-play score sheet.

Players compete to create rows of three symbols — either Xs or Os — in a shared 6x6 grid. On a turn, you roll the dice, then mark an empty space in the grid that matches the numbers rolled, e.g., if you roll 2-4, then you can place a symbol in the second space of the fourth column or the fourth space of the second column. If you can't place a symbol, you receive a penalty.

If you receive three penalties or create three lines of your symbols — with overlapping lines being permitted — then the game ends.

It is a simple game to score as much as possible points by rolling the dice. It is a game of luck and tactic. You can play it alone or with friends. You can download a score sheet for 2 players. But it’s also up to you if you want to do it different way and add the scores in the way you use to do it in any other game.

Basic game is played with 5 (d6) regular dice.
Determine a starting player by rolling 1-2 dice.
How to play:

On first roll every player must roll all 5 dice and keep the dice with the lowest number of dots (points)

Every roll player can keep only 1 dice and this dice should be higher than the previous dice that was left

If there are NO dice higher than the dice that was left before – attempt stops - stop rolling dice and count the score.

There could be maximum 5 rolls in attempt – that means every roll there was a higher dice left – for example:  1 > 3 > 4 > 5 > 6

First dice will give 1 point if it will be the only dice player could keep (doesn’t matter how many dots are there on it)

If player could keep 2 dice it gives him 2 points - doesn’t matter how many dots are there on the dice

If player kept 3 dice – he gets the same amount of points that has the third dice

If player kept 4 dice his points are the sum of 3rd and 4th dice

If player kept 5 dice his points are the sum of 3rd, 4th and 5th dice
family: Series: Free PnP during 2019-2020 COVID-19 Pandemic (Asmodee)

description:
A family of cowardly ogres, a journey to overcome their fears and the awareness that they must do it together.

These are just some of the elements of this cooperative game.
The children will have to move on the board and - by drawing "Courage" and "Talk about it" cards - overcome and beat the fears of the group: let's help each other and neutralize the different fears by telling and finding shared solutions.

Only by talking, interacting and collaborating the whole De Orchis family will reach the finish line and defeat the small and big daily fears that affect each of us.

Narration and cooperative play allow adults to gently approach the emotional world of the little ones and help them to express themselves by offering a lot of ideas on how many times it is possible to overcome their fears with ease, especially if you decide to share them.

The active player rolls the die and moves his token clockwise by as many squares as the result. Depending on the square where the movement ends, he draws a "Courage" card, a "Talk about it" card, a Fear card or immediately plays another turn. After moving the token, you can decide to use either a "Talk about it" or a "Courage" card to remove a Fear card in play. The turn passes to the next player.

You win all together only if you have removed all the Fear cards in play. If the players decide to stop the game, surrender, resign or run out of cards, the game is lost.

Labirintiny is a Fantasy themed Dungeon Crawling boardless boardgame, set in a neverending evershifting maze.
You are one of the four heroes lost in the maze. Each of you is unique: different abilities, powers, starting characteristics and capacities. But you all have the same aim: find the exit.
The first who finds it and makes it out alive causes the maze to collapse on the others, winning the game.

Labirintiny is a boardgame, but it's boardless. The "board" on which the players move is a maze formed by the cards. Each hero has its own particular start card, and every time a character steps "out of the board", a new card is placed in the maze.
Every card is unique, as every maze formed at every game.
It's practically impossible to play twice the same scenario.

Players move across the dungeon formed by the cards, looking for the exit. The dungeon is filled with monsters to defeat, chests to unlock, treasures to find, opportunities to seize, events that can change everything, and ways to obtain more objects and power.
The exits, even if found right away, are protected and difficult to pass through. Only the bravest will dare to challenge their defenses!

With Labirintiny you can: play alone against the maze; or with 2-4 players, each on their own; or 4 players paired in teams. In addition to these options there are different game modes included in the rules.
Another important aspect of the game is the size: the game adapts to the surface on which is played, and not vice-versa. You can play on a table, on the floor, on a chair, any surface as long as it's stable: your choice will determine the maximum size of the maze.

Self-produced by Mezzocielo&Friends,it's their first boardgame.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Campaign Trail: That Geek from the Media
description:
This promo candidate card was made available as part of the Game Boy Geek's 2020 Kickstarter campaign.

It has the following positive and negative special ability:

Place 1 voter from the undecided supply into the state in which you end your turn.

Use an undecided voter token on the track below. At the end of each of your turns move the token to the first action you took on that turn. You may not take this action as the first action on your next turn.

boardgame: Campaign Trail: Saxophone Serenade On Late Night TV
description:
This promo action card was made available as part of the Game Boy Geek's 2020 Kickstarter campaign.

It has the following Politick action:

Play immediately after taking your first action on your turn. Remove 1 voter of any opponent(s) from up to 3 cities (returning them to their party's registered supply) and replace each with 1 of your registered voters.

Flamme Rouge: Grand Tour introduces multi-stage campaigns, varying from short 3-stage Tours all the way up to full 21-stage Grand Tours.

In addition to the campaign mode, this expansion also includes new track tiles, special stages and specialist riders that allow you to customize your decks.

Each of these additions can be used both in single-stage races and in the campaign mode.

boardgame: 居酒屋オブザデッド～ゾンビ探しは注文のあとで～ (Izakaya of the Dead: Zombie Sagashi wa Chuumon no Ato de -)
description:
居酒屋オブザデッド～ゾンビ探しは注文のあとで～ (Izakaya of the Dead - Zombie Sagashi wa Chuumon no Ato de -) is a Japanese game about zombies at a bar. The title means 'Izakaya of the Dead - We'll Find Zombies after Making
Orders -'.

In the midst of a zombie panic, drunkards still want to drink. A zombie has slipped into the drunkards' drinking party. We must find the zombie or we'll be in danger! But making orders comes first. Let's start off with beer!

This is a social deduction game of a zombie and drunkards. Make orders from the menu with a growl and gesture with one hand. If they find the zombie or manage to make orders successfully, the drunkards win!

ゾンビパニック発生！ しかし、それでも酒を飲みたい酔っ払いたち。
酔っ払いの飲み会の中に１体、ゾンビが紛れ込んでしまった。ゾンビを見つけないと危険だ！
でも注文が先だ。とりあえず生！

ゾンビとよっぱらいの正体隠匿パーティーゲーム
唸り声と片腕ジェスチャーでメニューを注文
ゾンビを見つけるか注文成功でよっぱらいの勝利
publisher's summary

Put on your spookiest costume and go trick-or-treating in your street. Try to get the best candy while avoiding the disappointing treats. But don’t worry, once you get home you will have an opportunity to swap. Make sure you keep quiet though, you only have until mom and dad notice you’re home and send you to bed!

Trick or treat is a light-weight push your luck card game. You will be going door-to-door drawing candy cards at each house, aiming to get your favorite candy while avoiding those you have ranked the lowest. While you have some information about what is at the house, you can never be 100% sure what the next draw will turn up.

Careful though, as it isn't just bad candy you are risking by continuing to draw, but also the good will of your neighbors; drawing a matching event card will trigger a unique event at each house. You just took a chocolate bar, your favorite! Do you go back in for another and risk dropping the chocolate back into the bucket? The Grinch at the next house has told you to only take two. Do you listen, or keep drawing and hope he doesn't notice?

Once home you have until someone draws the parents card to trade your undesirable candy or tales of the evenings adventures for better candy. Whoever manages to get the best haul of candy (according to their personal ranking) wins.

—description from the designer

Imalya is a push your luck dice game for 2-4 players.
Each player takes on the role of one of 3 rival Factions participating in the fight against the Black Queen E’Dyrha: Assassins, Thieves and the Resistance. The players roll the dice and try to make combinations in order to be the first to reach 100 points.

Players start with 8 coins (called Tales). In turn, a player rolls the 6 (or 7) dice. They can decide to re-roll all or part of the dice as many times as they want, following the rules. When the player has decided to stop rolling the dice (or cannot anymore), they pay the Tribute for that turn: each E’Dyrha die in Prison and each Faction at the Lair other than the player's Faction cost 1 Tale. A player who cannot pay the Tribute is eliminated but keeps their score.

The player then proceeds to scoring his turn, calculating the number of the player's Faction dice multiplied by the total number of Faction dice in the Lair.

The games comes with 2 variants:

     The Cursed One: 1 more die is added to the roll, with new special effects on 3 of its sides.
     The Spells: players use a deck of 40 cards to spice up the game by getting new temporary abilities.


—description from the publisher

Take a side and win the perfection game using Biden and Trump nesting dolls depicting the Republicans and the Democrats. This limited-edition game set marks an important event 2020 in US history!
The aim of the game is to go through each stage using playing cards or dice. The winner of each stage puts nesting doll into the one next in size until reaches nesting doll with the White House
Fun, dynamic, and unpredictable board games for adults and family are already waiting for you. Players: 2. Ages: 8. Duration of the game: 10-20 minutes with playing cards or 5-10 minutes with dice
51 playing cards have been developed to simulate the U.S. electoral system for great gameplay in the card games for adults and families. Each card has 2 attributes: the number of points and the color
True Bid game will turn the political debate into real fun. When you play True Bid with friends and family, there is only one question posed – who will win the political battle and become president?

True Bid - 2020 Political Battle: fun board game with depicting Donald Trump and Joe Biden nesting dolls.

Who will occupy the Oval Office? The game will turn any political debate into real fun.

Features:

     Dynamic and unpredictable game;
     True Bid be appreciated by collectors;
     True Bid is a great keepsake;
     True Bid is also a great piece of decor.


—description from the designer

Atalanta football team themed Monopoly.

Atalanta Bergamasca Calcio is the football team of the city of Bergamo in Lombardy, Italy. Named after the Greek mythology heroine, the club has a long history since its founding in 1907.

Choose your token, place it on GO! and roll the dice to own it all! 
There can be only one winner in the Monopoly game. Will it be you?

Fast-dealing property trading game. Players buy, sell and trade properties to win. Build houses and hotels on properties. 
Change your fortune with Chance and Community Chest cards.
And bankrupt your opponents to win it all.

English:

On this remarkable night, bats fly by the hospital's facade. Creepy monsters limp by the corridors and frighten patients and staff alike. Add this spooky cards to your Virus! deck for a terrifying experience!

Spice up your VIRUS! games with new treatment cards (specific colour thieves, failed experiments, alien transplants, trick or treat) and the new mutant organ, to take your experience even further and delight at the thematic artwork!

—description from the publisher


Español:

Esta noche tan señalada, los murciélagos revolotean en la fachada del hospital. Monstruos aterradores recorren sus pasillos y atemorizan a pacientes y personal médico. ¡Añade estas terroríficas cartas a tu baraja de Virus! para una experiencia espeluznante!

¡Con las nuevas cartas de tratamiento (ladrones de colores, experimentos fallidos, trasplantes alienígenas, truco o trato...) y el nuevo órgano mutante conseguiréis dotar a vuestras partidas de Virus! de nuevas posibilidades y disfrutar de unas ilustraciones de miedo!

Phantom Doctrine is a co-operation espionage stealth board game that will let players take charge and manage a Spy Agency. Players will have to build a base, sneak around the enemy territory and steal information, rescue hostages and many more. To spice things up, Agents can turn out to be double agents that will cross you in the least expected moment. In downtime when preparing for missions players will upgrade their base and gear up Agents. Better base locations will let them buy better guns, train agents in the deadly art of killing and many more. Players will also send them on global missions that are necessary to push the storyline forward.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The StoryMaster's Tales: Deeper into the Woods
description:
This expansion contains:
Four new classes:
The Bard: Can avoid fight three time a day by rolling his magic or under.
The Wise Woman: Her fortune never goes below 2.
Lost Childrens: Play as two characters, or two players play one of each. The Brother is more proficient at fighting than her sister, who is more adept in magic.
The Gentleman: Starts with 100 gold, and can get 100 more by rolling a one at The Gates of Threatlore Town three time a day.

Four new quests:
Legend of the Hunt: Chosen One of the prophecy, you must face Odin's Wild Hunt.
A Fistful of Gold: You were drafted to do some voluntary militia works. Your mission: Kill the Bandit Lord.
The Eyes of the Past: After a vision of pestilence coming from an ancient Celtic barrow, the druid Mug Ruith confirms your vision, and instruct you with the task to bring back the Golden Skull to the tomb, where it belongs.
Path of the Forgotten: After what seemed a perfect day start, you find a sword that hold a message from his prior owner. He begs you to avenge him, for he was hanged without trials and is sure he was framed.

15 new story cards, with some of these connecting to The StoryMaster's Tales "Dracodeep Dungeon" Hybrid RPG.

10 new reward cards.

Seven alternative rules, including a rule for critical hit and critical failure, a rule for items with added damage, to add realism, a rule for dual wielding, and a rule to use the same character for multiple quests.

Two D6 dice (for the advanced combat rule).

Play the game for anyone with a neck! "SHAME, SHAME, SHAME, SHAME!" It's the chant heard 'round the table in the game where shame comes in the form of a cone you'll have to wear if you give the lamest answer. Grab up to five friends and have a laugh as you find out how well you know each other. Answer questions, act things out, draw pictures and avoid wearing the Cone of Shame! Once you’re wearing the Cone you’ll have to carry out challenges to escape and win your friends’ respect back. Go on.

—description from the publisher

Because people around the world are at risk from the virus, researchers turn to vaccine laboratories to develop vaccines.

Vaccine Lab is a dice, luck, risk, reasoning and co-op game for 2 to 4 players. Your goal is to show the actions of all players working together to develop a given vaccine. When luck comes, the pace of development gradually increases, and when bad luck comes, the goal cannot be achieved. When the turn comes, roll the dice to walk between luck and misfortune. Then he plays 1 card that affects all players.

You can draw or take a card depending on where you place it. This choice is watched by all players.

Can you develop a vaccine in an urgent situation where time is tight?

<Purpose of the game>
1. You need to develop a vaccine within the number of players +1 round.

<Game ready>
1. After shuffling the cards, all players draw 2 cards. The remaining cards are placed in a pile in the center of the table.
2. Starting with the first player, take 3 antivirus chips out of their pockets and cover them with their hands so that others cannot see them. If all players have their anti-virus chips side-by-side, put their hands away at the same time and reveal them to everyone. It is both a viral particle and a vaccine to be developed.
3. The cards in the pile are used to make 2 sectors. The sector puts 4 cards next to the pile. This place is called a laboratory.

    Even if the order of the vaccine chips and cards is the same during the game preparation process, the vaccine was not developed, and it is successful only when the new card placed by the player has the same order of the cards in the lab.


4. Place the round chips next to the pile.
(Round Chip = Number of Players +1)

<Game play>
1. On your turn, roll the dice.
2. If the scale of the rolled dice is on the nearest card in the pile, turn it over.
3. Select 1 card from your hand and place 1 card at the end of the desired sector.
4. Make sure a vaccine has been developed.
5. If the order of the vaccine to be developed and the potion card between team members are the same, the vaccine for virus treatment has been successfully developed. Store successfully developed vaccines aside.
6. Place a card and draw a card from the front of the sector or from the pile. In this way, it preserves 4 cards per sector.
(Please push the cards toward the pile so the next player can place them.)

    If you place a card and the card in front of the sector is turned over, remove the card and unconditionally draw a card from the pile.


7. At the end of the turn, the next player proceeds.

<Game ends>
1. Researcher wins if everyone succeeds in developing a vaccine after a +1 player number round. But if even one person fails to develop a vaccine, researchers are defeated.

“Trasteros Locos. Crazy Auctions” is a game in which players become collectors of amazing collections of a specific theme. Every collection consists of 10 different items with different values and the player´s goal is to obtain the most valuable collection of their type. Their role will be secret during the game-play and they will simulate going to the storage facility to bid for abandoned storage rooms or access to the public storage room.

They will also need to use wisely their resources to allow them getting cards or increase their number of cubes and their value.  The game includes six different collectors with their respective collections. On their turn a player can make one action of seven different ones.

At the end of the game the player with most points will be the winner of the game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Victory at Sea: IJN Submarines & MTB sections
description:
Submarines: The Kaidai-7-class, or KD-7, was developed in the late 1930s, following on from the preceding KD-6 class. With a surface range of 8,000nm at 16kts, and a submerged endurance of 50nm at 5kts, they possessed a slightly better underwater performance to the late-model KD-6s and better surface speed (though not range) than the earlier members of that class. All members of the class were lost by mid-1944. Another ocean-going submersible using 533mm forward and aft torpedo tubes, it served in the South Pacific as well as the waters off Australia. I-177 sunk the hospital ship AHS Centaur off Stradbroke Island. It was presumed lost with all hands on 18 November 1944, while the I-176 was lost six months earlier off of the Solomon’s. There were 10 KD-7 submarines built overall.

MTBs: Designed as a suicide craft, the Shin’yō either carried a powerful explosive or two depth charges. The depth charges were intended to be planted by the pilot of the boat, after which he would then make his escape. Although nearly 10,000 were built, they accounted for the sinking of only 5 ships, mainly landing craft.

The Imperial Japanese Navy fielded a number of variants on a standard torpedo boat design, differing mainly in machinery fit which affected displacement, giving the illusion of more variety than existed in practice. 238 boats were built within these designations, all armed with two 18-inch torpedoes and 25mm or 13.2mm guns.

Contents:
3 Kaidai-7-class Submarine
2 Shin'yo Kamikaze boat sections
2 T-14 MTB sections
Ship Cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Victory at Sea: US Navy Submarines & MTB sections
description:
Submarines: The Gato-class of submarine was the first mass production US submarine class of the Second World War, forming the majority of the United States Navy’s submarine fleet of the war. It was the Gato-class, and the successors of her design that were largely responsible for the disruption of the Japanese merchant fleet. Individual Gato-class vessels were given names of marine creatures, with the lead of her class named for a type of small catshark.

MTBs: The Elco mounted four torpedo tubes which, combined with its speed, made it a serious threat to larger ships. The largest PT boat used by the US Navy, the Elco is also notable for future President Kennedy commanding one. Crews of these boats relied on their smaller size, speed and manoeuvrability – and darkness – to survive.

Contents:
3 Gato-Class Submarines
2 Elco ‘77 PT Boat Sections
2 Elco ‘80 PT Boat Sections
Ship Cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Victory at Sea: Kriegsmarine U-Boats & MTB sections
description:
Submarines: A long-ranged submersible, the Type IX was the most successful U-boat of the war, with each vessel averaging over 100,000 tons of shipping sunk. One Type IX, U-107, made the most successful convoy mission of the war, with nearly 100,000 tons of shipping sunk out of Freetown in Africa. The latest variants of this design were capable of ranges of more than 23,000 miles, allowing them to rove far in search of convoys, while their heavy load of torpedoes allowed them to keep pace with a convoy, attacking night after night.

MTBs: The R1-class of R-Boat (from the German Räumboot, meaning minesweeper) was intended to be used as a shallow water minesweeper but, as the war went on, it became a multi-role craft. Its duties expanded to include patrol, antisubmarine, convoy escort, minelaying, and rescue operations. Some classes of R-Boat, such as the R310, were fitted with torpedo tubes, though performance was very modest compared to craft specifically designed for the role.

The ultimate S-Boat to be operational in significant numbers was the S-100-class, which was produced from 1943 and was said to be the best fast patrol boat of its time. The S-100-class was nicknamed the Calotte, as it featured a rounded armoured bridge. It was powered by three Daimler-Benz engines giving it an overall capacity of around 7,500 hp and developing an outstanding top speed of 48 knots.

Contents:
3 Type IX U-Boats
2 S-100 S-Boat Sections
2 R-Boat Sections
Ship Cards

—description from the publisher

Did you know that there are rules about garbage disposal? 
Collections are board games that connect natural flows while saving value and money by choosing and giving up opportunities in the process of exchange, collection, sale and resource conversion.

<Purpose of the game>
1. Let's collect recycling, separate collection, and save money.

<Game ready>
1. After shuffling the cards evenly, each player takes 5 cards and 3 tokens.
The remaining cards are placed in a deck in the middle of the table.

When it's your turn, you take 4 actions in turn. The choice of each situation has a great influence on future variables.

<Game play>
1. Card exchange (1 to 2 cards) or separate collection vehicle operation
Choose a player and trade cards. (1 or 2)
If no one wants to exchange, proceed to the next action.
Separate collection vehicles must be operated with the cards they are collecting.
When the car runs, everyone else discards the card from the collected cards.

2. Collection Card Resource Exchange
Cards collected in the previous round can be exchanged for tokens.
(Cards played in this round can be sold after 1 turn.)

3. Card collection (up to 3 types)
You can collect up to 3 types of sorting bins.
What you collect can be sold in the next round.
If you have a disposable recycling bin card, you can collect up to 4 cards.

4. Card replenishment (5 sheets stored)
Fill in the cards used in the deck.
When your turn is over, you must have 5 cards in your hand.

5. If you have executed the actions in order, the turn is passed to the next person.

-When you want to discard a card you are collecting, you pay 2 tokens to the bank for a penalty of unauthorized speculation and discard 1 type of card you choose.
(Cards in your hand cannot be discarded.)

-If you are holding a disposable bin, you can collect additional cards of different types.
After the collection is over, when you sell the cards you are collecting in this bin, you will also discard the disposable recycling bin card.

<Game ends>
1. When someone draws the last card of the deck in the center of the table, the game ends by progressing one by one starting with the next player. Collect all the cards in your hand by type and exchange 3 or more cards for tokens. If you have a garbage truck, you can get 1 token per card. The player with the highest total token score wins.

boardgame: Tiny Ninjas: Original – The Void Expansion
description:
The Void adds 12 new ninja cards to Tiny Ninjas.

Including all new characters: Queen, Skeleton, Mystic, Zombie, Dragon and also introducing YELLOW DAMAGE to the game!

—description from the publisher

-- Second Edition --

The Void Expansion adds 12 new Ninja Cards to Tiny Ninjas: Original

Includes: Queen, Mystic, Dragon, Zombie Solo, Zombie Archer, Zombie Master, and Zombie Panda.

Also introducing YELLOW DAMAGE to the game!
Yellow Damage can only be defended by a Yellow Shield.

(NOTE: THIS IS NOT A STANDALONE PRODUCT. Requires a copy of 'Tiny Ninjas: Heroes' to play).

CONTENTS:
4 HERO Dice, 10 HERO Cards, 24 ITEM Cards, 1 Rules Card

New HEROES: 
Vampire, Princess, Alien, Dragon

Bonus HERO:
Angry Kitty (Hero Cards only)

New ITEMS: 
Golden Shield, Trio's Blades, Angel's Feathers, Grim's Scythe, Gambler's Coin, Pirate's Chest, Medic's Kit, Magician's Hat, Staminana, Grappling Hook, Energy Core, Shadow's Shuriken, Helping Heart, Poison, Fire Scroll, Robot's Robobytes, Brawlcoli, Golden Shuriken

Soccer Brain
An outstanding new family game designed to improve a variety of Thinking Skills. 
The game begins with a 'cup draw' of eight teams who round by round play the quarter finals, semi-finals and final. With five minutes per half, each player moves across the board, tackling various football-related thinking challenges as they go. There are four kinds of challenge

     memory test,
     visual perception,
     lateral thinking and
     Conundrums


If you're lucky, you'll win a penalty and have the chance to 'score from the spot'. 
Great fun in a game that really will improve thinking skills!

Master the laws of nature by controlling the elements! Teleport into the astral dimension and choose to control Huo (fire), Shui (water) or Tu (earth). Use your special powers to atomise or fuse your element and create the perfect sequence of five. The winner restores balance to our chaotic universe. So, Spin away and Wu Xing!

Xing is a game of strategy and balance where players are the masters of a single element. They must use the language of the universe - Math - to tactically position their elements in a sequence and gain victory over others. Spin, pick a card and solve the question to find your position(s) on the board. Place your element token using strategy and planning. Master basic concepts of factors and multiples and gain critical thinking skills in this epic battle of minds!

In Xing, players master the laws of nature by controlling the elements - Huo, Shui and Tu. Each player picks one element and collects 14 tokens of that element. The game board and action spinner are placed in the centre.
Shuffle the fuse and atomise card decks separately and place them beside the game board. 
On your turn, spin the action spinner.
If you get Fuse, pick the top card from the fuse deck. 
If you get Atomise pick the top card from the atomise deck and,
If you get Pick Any, pick the top card from any deck.
After you pick a card, read what is said and solve it. then choose where you want to place one of your element tokens 
For example, if the card reads, ‘multiple of 4 between 35 to 55’, then you can place your token on 36, 40, 44 or 48.

Remember, you can place only one of your element tokens per turn
You must place the element based on the solution of the card you picked.
If the card has more than one solution, pick one of them and place your token there
If a location already has a token then you cannot place another
If you cannot place your token on the game board, you pass your turn

In each deck, there are 2 Wu (the spiral symbol) cards and 2 remove cards. These are special cards. 
The Wu card allows you to place one token on the wu symbol on the board. The ‘remove’ card allows you to remove one token of an opponent from the board. when a token is removed it goes back to that player. Once all your element tokens have been used, then you can move them around on the board. just take it from one spot and place it on another, as per the card you pick. Remember, you can place an element token to create your sequence or block an opponent. choose your strategy! The first player to create a line of five of their own element tokens, wins the game. This could be horizontal, vertical or diagonal. Xing can also be played with 6 players split into 3 teams of 2.

—description from the publisher

Pack your bags, get ready… we’re going on an adventure! We’ll travel through the dense jungle of Amazonia, the scorching desert of Tenere, the icy mountains of Makalu and the fiery skies of Shangri La to collect mystical treasures. But beware, perils lie everywhere. Anyone can enter but only a few can escape…

Trailblazers is an exciting memory drawing game that your family will not get enough of. The goal is simple: see a map, spot the treasures and obstacles, remember them and then draw your own way out of it using memory and some basic rules. Then, spin away and maximise your points! Sounds simple but is a laughing riot when opponents take a wrong turn. 
Enjoy as your kids use principles of geometry, memory and strategy to draw their way through various adventure worlds. See them master basic concepts of lines and angles, decision making and motor skills right before your eyes!

In Trail Blazers, players travel through four different adventures, collecting coins and avoiding obstacles. The goal is to draw your way out of each maze in the adventure region. Everybody starts with a Path Map, and a sketch pen. Place the adventure book in the centre. Choose an adventure region, and start with Maze 1. Cover the maze with the Spin-A-Round spinner. Keep your sand timer ready.
VIEWING ROUND (30 secs) - When the timer begins, all players must visualise and remember a path in the maze from Start to Finish. 
During this time, players are not allowed to draw anything. All pens must be down!
At the end of 30 seconds, the maze is covered again with the Spin-A-Round spinner.
DRAWING ROUND (60 secs) - All players draw their paths on their Path Maps from Start to Finish. They must try to score maximum points by collecting coins and avoiding obstacles. All players must enter with a Ray at the Start point and exit with a Ray at the Finish point. You can enter or exit horizontally or vertically but not diagonally. All paths must be individual line segments, connecting the dots on the path maps from Start to Finish. All paths must be horizontal or vertical and not diagonal. At the end of the drawing round, each player counts their score by placing their path map on the maze in the adventure book. Each coin you collect in your path, you get the points mentioned on it. Each obstacle you hit, you lose points as mentioned on the left hand pages. Add all points for the coins collected, subtract all points for the obstacles hit, add 2 points for entering with a ray at the correct Start point and add 2 points for exiting with a ray at the correct Finish point. This is your score for the drawing round. 
SPINNING ROUND - The player with the highest score for this maze, spins the spinner. Now, choose either your entry or exit Ray as the reference Ray. If you get a Parallel, then you count all lines parallel to the reference Ray. If you get a Perpendicular, then you count all lines perpendicular to the reference Ray. Then you multiply the number above by either x1 or x2 as per what they have spun. This is your score for the spinning round. 
Add the scores of the Drawing and Spinning round as the final score for maze 1. Repeat  the same for Mazes 2 and 3 in the adventure region. The person with the highest total of all three mazes, wins!

—description from the publisher

Kuih Muih is a card game about collecting Malaysian delicacies. It is played over three stages, in which players collect cards to score points. At every stage, cards are divided into stacks. Each round, players take turns to claim a stack, then pick a card  from it. Unclaimed cards are returned to the table, with players now knowing the contents of the stack just seen. When a new round starts, player order changes, and players may go for the same stack or try a different stack.

Cards score points in different ways. Usually the more cards of a kind you have the better. Some cards have special powers, e.g. allowing you to swap cards with an opponent.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
6 exclusive cards for backers of the Fantastic Factories: Manufactions Kickstarter. They can be simply added to the Manufactions expansion.

2 D.R.A.W. Studio cards
2 Wood Workshop cards
2 Mimic cards - helps you out by copying the effect of any contractor in the marketplace
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
2 Card promo pack that was part of the Fantastic Factories: Manufactions Kickstarter. They can be simply added to the Manufactions expansion.

Metafactory Industries - its power is to copy other faction powers.

BIG Pharma is a corporate faction that really rewards the usage of vitamins in a big way. Instead of changing the value of a die by 1, vitamins can now also change the value of a die by 2! The challenge here is that you don’t start the game with any way to gain vitamins. Luckily you get to see 9 blueprint cards and get 3 vitamins to start the game.

boardgame: Quest for the Lost Pixel: Catacombs of Atheria
description:
In Catacombs of Atheria, the game now throws in a fun press-your-luck mini game known as the Catacombs. You will need to manage your energy to get in and out of there before you pass out and lose what you earned.

The Catacombs is not the only new addition to the game. This expansion also includes more customization for your heroes. Now when your Hero levels up to a certain level, they will have the choice between 2 (Hero specific) additional classes giving them new abilities.

Speaking of Heroes, the expansion also comes with 2 new heroes - The Bard and The Summoner.

Also, new dungeon tiles, events, and artifact cards are added for more things to find, equip, and explore. Why make the Lost Pixel your only quest? Now I have introduced 20 Side Quests to the game. You will be given 3 random Side Quests at the beginning of the game so you can gain more Items and XP as you accomplish each.

Lastly, now each floor of the dungeon comes with 1 Boss Monster. These monsters are devastating, but if you are willing to fight and defeat them, the reward is great!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Quest for the Lost Pixel: Risk and Reward
description:
In Risk and Reward, you are given brand new choices to push your luck for great rewards. Everything in this expansion leaves you with difficult choices. Plus this expansion offers a reinforcement set of monsters, treasures, spells, events and more. Lastly, it has a few treasure fixes that replace old cards.

Risk and Rewards offers new Dungeon Areas for your heroes to explore. But why risk the time into exploring these new areas? BRAND NEW SET TREASURES. These get better the more of the same set that you have. Unfortunately, each new area can be very dangerous as well. Also, you will receive PETS that will give you a permanent buff until you decide on discarding your pet for their one time great ability. Lastly, there are 5 new heroes to choose from.

NEW HEROES: Blood Mage, Psionic, Spell Blade, Time Bender, and the Duelist.

Lastly, almost all decks receives tons of new cards from monsters and events to treasures and artifacts. Plus so much more.

—description from the publisher

Rubik's Perplexus Hybrid is two puzzles united in a mobile labyrinth: a unique Perplexus coupled with a Rubik's Cube! You have to rotate the faces horizontally and vertically to recreate the path of the labyrinth and move the ball forward in 3D and by rotation. Finishing the labyrinth allows you to finish the Rubik's Cube. In all, it has 100 obstacles to overcome!
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
The objective is simple: Be the first player to arrange four of your game pieces in a two-by-two square. But winning this clever two-person game requires both a keen memory and cunning strategy.

There are 10 total player pieces: 3 orange, 3 green, and 4 two-color pieces.

Setup:
Turn five of the pieces orange side up and five pieces green side up, which will ensure the right distribution of two-color pieces. Each player then chooses a color and takes their five pieces.

Moves:
Player 1 starts by selecting a piece to play, after looking underneath to check its color, and places the piece anywhere on the board. Player 2 can then decide to PLACE one of their pieces on the board, FLIP any pieces on the board over while keeping it in the same space, or MOVE any piece of their color to a different space (you cannot move a piece that is not your color, only flip it).

Winning:
The game continues while each player tries to wager between placing, flipping, or moving the pieces on the board to create a two-by-two square of their color.

There is a commotion in the royal palace. In the midst of the crowds, the king wants to ascend the throne and establish order. On the other side is the peasant who wants to expose the hand of the king and his agents. Reaching the throne is not that simple as sometimes it is necessary to enter the dressing room and change clothes so as not to be recognized by the opponent.

In King Thief Minister: Duel, the King, the Thief, the Minister, the Sherif, the Executioner, and the Peasant move in the form of cups on a map of the palace and hide from each other.  Ultimately, it is the memory contest that determines the winner of the game.

boardgame: Chronicles of Drunagor: Age of Darkness – The Shadow World
description:
The Heroes of Drunagor struggle mightily against the Darkness to keep the light of hope alive. But beyond the edge of reality there sits another world—one where Darkness has already claimed dominion over all! Dare to bring the light to this Shadow World and face horrifying creatures of pure evil. But beware: for wherever there is light, there are Shadows!

Age of Darkness is a fully-cooperative strategy board game of tactical combat and dungeon-crawl style adventure for one to five players. Explore the world of Drunagor through the game’s immersive 3d environment and illustrated Points of Interest—where your choices determine the route your story will take! You need a copy of the Chronicles of Drunagor:  Age of Darkness core game to play with this expansion.

The ballads of heroism are being told.

As narrators of this story in The Ballad of Heroes, we can constantly change the story so that the greatness and glory go to the hero that our tribe wants. In this way, we do not neglect hand-to-hand combat.

Ahriman warriors stand in front of an old mansion that has been hidden underground for centuries. According to legend, they know that the mansion is protected by the Ahriman. They enter through the main entrance, but when the last of them has entered, they notice that the trap doors begin to slide shut. A voice in the mansion shouts, "You are buried here forever."

When you find yourself inside The Dark Mansion, a one-vs-all game, you might consider it a grave, but as one of the fighters, you have tools on hand that can help you survive. Can you defeat the devil and get out of the mansion?
A 6-card expansion of additional extinct animals, that have special eating requirements and no special abilities. You can shuffle these with the animals in the base game.

Walk 500 miles without also walking 500 more.

Inspired on the one hit wonder song I'm Gonna Be (500 Miles) by The Proclaimers.

It's John du Bois month here, and we start with a game about traveling on a map, kind of. :) You will be flicking pennies along the edge of the postcard to walk 500 miles before your opponent. I also have to mention that this was our first time working with Maricica Cârstiuc and her art is wonderful! It really captured the whimsical tone we were hoping for, and I'd love to work with her again!

—description from the publisher


A postcard dexterity game, where players race flickering pennies.

The first player, on their first turn, uses a finger to flick or push their penny so that it touches or crosses the first line - but not the second line. On each player's subsequent turns, the player flicks their penny so that it is closer to the right of the card that the other player's penny but does not completely cross two different lines on the same flick. If unsuccessful, the get one chance to retry.

If a player is unable to successfully complete their turn on a retry, they lose the game. If a player is able to flick their penny past the 500 mark without breaking the other rules for their turn, they win the game.


Released in the May 2017 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.
family: Series: One-Hit Wonder Songs postcards (Button Shy)

description:
Shake out your arms and get loosened up!

Remember Dead or Alive? Maybe not, but you´ll definitely know the song. We first saw this game being played at a variety of conventions (PAX, Unpub, etc), where a bunch of people were standing around a table, spinning the postcard, hugging each other, wagging their hands around and snapping their fingers. This game has you playing each line of the song, and laughing the whole way through.

—description from the publisher


Spin Me Right Round! is a moving and action game played in real time, so be ready!

The DJ will toss the postcard onto the table by spinning it in the air. The action for that round is determined by whichever corner is pointing at the DJ when the postcard stops spinning. Once the DJ calls out the action, all Dancers must perform that action as fast as they can, then slap the postcard.

The first Dancer to slap the postcard will be the DJ for the next round, and the last one is eliminated from the game.

Once down to only 2 players, whoever slaps first, wins!


Released in the April 2017 Board Game of the Month Club $5+ package.

description:
A quick Tetris-inspired roll-and-write game.

Try to both score valuable rows and snatch bonuses before your opponent takes them. Plays in about 20 min and is designed for for 1 or 2 players.

On your turn you roll all four dice. The majority of the faces tell you which shape you have to enter into your grid with the values of the rolled dice. You score points for completed rows and for completing bonus tasks.

Once a year, the renowned Ludikah School of Witchcraft choose a student to turn them into a great wizard. The apprentice that displays their magical skills will be the chosen one!
Cast spells by mixing potions correctly and by using some magical items. Be careful! Some spells may have unexpected effects and they may bring chaos among the novice wizards.
MAGUS NOVATUS is a family game with the perfect combination of decision, chance and interaction. It guarantees a great fun to all players in their role of novice wizards.

The goal in MAGUS NOVATUS is to be the player with the most point at the end of the game. In order to do so, players will try to get as many Spell cards as possible, which will grant 2, 3 or 4 points depending on their difficulty.

The components the boardgame are cards, dice and Measuring Cylinder marker.

MAGUS NOVATUS is a boardgame for 2-4 players, 30 minutes and +8 years old.

Una vez al año, la prestigiosa escuela de magia Ludikah elige a un alumno para convertirlo en un gran mago. Aquel aprendiz que demuestre sus dotes mágicas será el elegido. 
Realiza hechizos combinando las pociones de forma correcta y haciendo uso de algunos objetos mágicos. ¡Pero cuidado! Algunos hechizos pueden tener efectos inesperados y desencadenar el caos entre los magos novatos.

Magus Novatus es un juego para toda la familia con la mezcla perfecta de decisión, azar e interacción que asegurará una gran diversión a los jugadores en su papel de magos novatos.
El objetivo en MAGUS NOVATUS es ser el jugador con más puntos al final de la partida. Para ello, los jugadores intentarán conseguir todas las cartas de hechizo posibles, las cuales  otorgarán 2, 3 o 4 puntos según su dificultad.

El juego está compuesto por cartas, un dado y una probeta para marcador de duración de la partida.

MAGUS NOVATUS es un juego para 2-4 jugadores, de 30 minutos de duración y para jugadores a partir de 8 años de edad.
