The game takes place with the roll of a dice that moves us forward on the board. Through the house where we fall, we exchange our cards for others of the same color, changing the title of our bizarre news.
When we hit the "Está na Hora" house, we started to invent our story based on the title we were building in front of us.
We have time counted by an hourglass and if we get away with it within that time and we manage to convince the other players, we win a prestigious coin.
At the end of the game the player with the most prestigious coins wins.

Van Gogh is a game in which we are the inspirations inside the artist's mind, the minigoghs (meeples), beings with different personalities and special skills: happy, dreamy, impatient, melancholic and playful minigoghs.

The meeples rolling mechanism reflects van gogh's untimely, fickle and creative personality, combined minigoghs can be allocated by their positions: lying, on its sides, standing or upside down.

Our goal will be to use the minigoghs in the most efficient way to paint the works, scoring points of prestige.
In the round, players take turns selecting a character card from those on the table receiving a full bonus. After this, every player to receive the simple bonus of the character.

Each space allocation is scored immediately and the winner will be the one who collaborates most efficiently with the paintings at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

War of the Realms is an asymmetric, tactical card game for two players that embraces RTS and TBS games from the past with nostalgic pixel art!

In the game, cards can be played by discarding required number of other cards from hand. Units can be sent to attack enemy realm or defend the player's own realm. The player can also build structures, play events or play upgrade cards. The player can also upgrade their realm, if required cards for next level are in play.

The combat is resolved with dice rolls. After dice result and modifiers are added, the result is compared to an enemy’s armor values. Then the corresponding damage is inflicted.

The game ends if a player destroys an opponent's realm. Alternatively, the game can be won if a realm is upgraded to level 3.

—description from the designer

boardgame: North South East Quest: Beyond Promo Card
description:
A promotional direction card for North South East Quest.


A direction card to take the game to other worlds! Mars? Tatooine? Middle Earth? The possibilities are endless.

If you really want to pull out all of the stops, you can take your story into SPACE! How you get there? That's the hard part. Use the hot air balloon? The train? A boat? You need to get REALLY creative with this one.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Düstere Zeiten – Zwei junge Bewahrer
description:
Promo for Die verschollenen Legenden: Düstere Zeiten

It is a single card expansion. A camp is placed on the board at the start of the game. One hero has to stay in the camp at the end of the day to protect the two keepers. Otherwise all heroes will loose two willpower. The heroes may gather equipment for the keepers so they can protect themselves. Then a hero will gain 2 willpower at the end of the day if he stays in the camp.

It was offered at the Kosmos online shop during the Spiel.digital 2020.

Players in Hunting Me, Hunting You secretly dish out damage in order to hunt their target. They must also pay attention to where and when damage was dealt to be able to identify who it is that's hunting them!

After Target Cards are dealt out by the all-knowing 'Curator' role player begins, starting with the appointed 'Leader' (a role which changes every phase).
Play takes place over two phases, which repeat until the round is over - The Night Phase and The Day Phase.

During The Night Phase players take turns as the only player with their eyes open - placing up to two damage cubes on other players' character cards. As each player 'wakes up' they will see where damage has been placed but not necessarily who did what.

The Day Phases sees players doing one of three actions:


     Reloading - collecting more cubes, up to a maximum of two.
     Spying - looking at another player's Target Card.
     Identifying - accusing another player of being their attacker.


When identifying, players announce who they think has them as their target. If they are correct the accused player dies and is out of the round (not the game). If they are wrong it is instead they who die!

Games are played in tournaments, in which players compete to win the most coins - with players getting paid after every round based on what they achieved.

It's a game about trickery, deduction and killing your friends for money. 5-8 players, with a 4 player variant.

—description from the designer

Your favorite Geishas are back with more talent and graceful performances! With the Hanamikoji action tiles expansions, you may play this award-winning 2-player game with even more tactics! These expansions contain different tiles combination. Mix and match with the previous action tiles for a more unique gaming experience!

Contains: #1 戀愛 "to be in love"
Discard 1
Secret 2
Transmit 3
Gift 4

Mord bei Tisch is a crimi dinner game in which 6-8 players can participate to find out who among them is the murderer of the found victim. In addition to that, every player gets a personal task to fulfil during the evening, which can include finding certain information via asking, hiding certain information during the game, convince other players to join them in certain acts or act in a very specific way during the game.

The backstage case is about the murder on the manager of the famous pop diva Miss Loco.

In this exciting game, players must collect complete sets of Parable Cards. You can draw as many cards as you like, but be careful. Draw an Oopsie! Card and you will pay the price! Will you be bold and fearless? Or will you be conservative and cautious?

Jesus was a masterful storyteller. Using common objects like sheep and seeds, Jesus told short but powerful parables to help His audience grasp important spiritual truths. This game features 12 of Jesus’ parables.

The objective of the game is to form complete sets of Parable Cards. Each set comprises 4 cards of the same parable and is identified by a colored title bar on the top-left of the cards. The first player to form 2 complete sets of Parable Cards wins.

You and two of your close friends are lost in nightmarish woods. You don’t remember how you got here… You will have to survive and desperately search for a way out. All whilst trying to avoid the numerous horrors that lurk in this never ending forest.

Into the Outside: Prelude is a Roll & Write mashed with a Choose Your Own Path story. Push your luck scavenging for food and wood to meet your survival needs. All whilst risking encountering the numerous monstrosities that hunt in these dark woods! Manage your camp, craft items, and level up skills to aid you in the Outside. Then with all your skills and tools at your side, Roll & Write your way through each scenario campaign’s branching narrative, culminating in one of various endings!

Into the Outside: Prelude contains one storybook scenario. This scenario comprises a 3 game campaign and variant for playing.

—description from the designer

In Flotter Dreier, each round you flip a red and a blue card, which determines the category. The goal for each player is, to provide three answers for this category. Ideally those answers are the most mentioned answers from the other players. The more identical answers, the more points for all the players involved. But beware, you don't want all of the players to give the same answer, since then, no one will score.

boardgame: That's it!: Which do you like?
description:
Everyone likes different things, sees things differently.
Can you pick a theme, then split everyone evenly?
Get them to choose exactly 50/50 to score points!
Let’s find out what they really like!
You can play at Online, and you can play with the listeners with the Streaming site.

Each player has one Voting card.
You can see A and B at face-up.

The turn player reads the top of the Topic card.
For example, the Italian food.

The turn player makes two selection.
A: Arrabbiata
B: Seafood pizza

Each player included in the turn player votes A or B secretly, and open all at once. *You cannot take a lie.

If 50/50, the turn player takes the Topic card as a score point.

At the end of the game, the winner is the player with the highest points.

You will know the secret liking of the players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: That's it!: Deep – Which do you like?
description:
Everyone likes different things, sees things differently.
Can you pick a theme, then split everyone evenly?
Get them to choose exactly 50/50 to score points!
Let’s find out what they really like!

You can play online, or with listeners on the streaming site.

This game is only for Japanese OTAKU. Because of many Japanese idioms and similar in the game, this edition will not be translated into English, and is only available in Japanese.

Each player has one Voting card, with an A on one side, and B on the other.

The lead player reads the top of the topic card for example, Animation. They then make two selections, for example A: Dragon Ball or B: One Piece.

Each player included in the round then votes A or B secretly, and all votes are revealed at once. Players may not lie about their selections.

If the votes are 50/50, the lead player takes the topic card and scores a point.

At the end of the game, the winner is the player with the most points.

You will all learn what the secret likings of the players are!

Let's learn multiplication tables! Let's learn multiplication tables!

'Dice99' is a functional educational board game that you can learn easily and enjoyably by multiplying the combinations of cards you have.

There are two conditions to remember when creating a multiplication table.

The first is to create the result of multiplying the numbers in the dodecahedron.
Second, you need to generate the resulting value with 2 cards of different colors.

Shout the multiplication table faster than anyone else, and who is the multiplication champion?

<Purpose of the game>
1. You should collect the most chips.

<Game ready>
1. The player deals 5 cards. The remaining cards are stacked face down and placed in the center of the table.
2. Place chips and dodecahedron dice next to the deck of cards.
3. Take one multiplication table and refer to it during the game.

<Game play>
1. The youngest rolls the dice.
(After that, the person who got the house by matching the correct answer rolls the dice.)
2. Check the dice, make the multiplication table result with 2 color cards and place it on the pile where the multiplication table shouts out loud.
3. If the dice side is ★, make the desired multiplication phrase result, shout the multiplication phrase aloud, and play 2 cards of different colors.
4. If the die is 3579, make an odd number using two cards of different colors.
5. If the dice is 2468, two cards of different colors are used to make an even number.
6. The player with the first result gets 1 chip. If several people produce results at the same time, the one who gets the most takes the chips. 
(You can return 1 chip, discard as many cards as you like, and draw them from the pile.)
7. The player with the chip replenishes 2 cards from the pile and then rolls the dice.

<Game ends>
1. When all the chips are gone, the game is over, and the player with the most chips wins.

The long awaited Christmas. Santa's kids are up for it again this year!
Take the presents on a sled at the factory and deliver them to cities around the world.
Many happenings are waiting for you on the way.
Who will collect the most bells, a sign of gratitude, before the morning arrives?

To Win:
Whoever collects the most bells at the end of the night wins.

How to play:
The rules of Santa Kids are very simple.
There are only four actions to choose from.
They are easy to understand: Grab a present, Move, Distribute a present, and Get a reindeer or sled.
Play as many of these actions as you have reindeer, and your turn is up.
Give out presents of the same color as the socks in each city, and collect as many bells as you can!
Fun events occur while you're playing! It's snowing and a nice wind. All sorts of happenings, like being called by Papa Santa and secret loopholes, make the game so much more exciting!

—description from the publisher
designers: Hiroyuki Sadamatsu (さだまつ ひろゆき)
publishers: Studium Mundi (スタジオムンディ)

description:
Potato Country has a lot of nice and unique potatoes, but they all dream of becoming the best star Potato.

Known to rise and fall in value with the weather, potatoes can grow into the best star potatoes by carefully managing them as the weather changes throughout the week.

Could your potato be the best star?

<Purpose of the game>
1. Out of the 7 character potatoes, the player who gets the most points by devising a strategy to reach the highest score for the potato he supports wins.

<Game play>
1. Place each potato character in position 0 on the board.
2. All players take 7 Weather Cards per color.
3. Shuffle the'Porta Star' candidate registration card and divide it into two. At this time, the character card taken once cannot be replac
4. Check the character card and turn it over.
5. Declare one, two, three and place the weather card face down.
6. Play the cards laid out in order of play.
7. Move the character according to the weather indicated on the card. At this time, you need to move the number of cards and the number of moving spaces at once.
8. When all players finish applying the effect according to the card, turn clockwise to play the game.

<Game ends>
1. The game ends when all weather cards are used. Reveal the character card and calculate the score. If there are 2 same characters, the score is doubled. However, if there are 2 in the minus, it is minus 2 times. The player with the highest score wins by adding up the scores of each character he has.

The game name pronunciation in Japanese is タングルウィード (Tangleweed) which translates into Tumbleweed. (The publisher gives the full title as タングルウィード～緑をかけぬけうまれる種～ (Tanguruuido-midori wo kakenuke umarerutane-) meaning Tangleweed - the seed that runs through the greenery -).

Welcome to the West. As a tumbleweed farmer you want to become rich by growing your tumbleweed.  Roll the tumbleweed down the land, and let it grow.  Gain more money so you can buy more land.

At the end of the 7th round, the farmer who earns the most with the tumbleweed seeds through the final market price of the tumbleweed is the winner.

This is a engine-building game to grow the weeds that you often see tumbling in the wind in Western films. In the world where Western films are all the rage, there is a surge in demand for tumbleweeds!

You have started a new career as tumbleweed farmers. Grow and sell tumbleweeds to make a fortune! Read the wind and make the weeds tumble as they drop seeds on their path! Make use of the help effectively to grow rich!

The players lay tiles to expand their farmlands. The tumbleweeds (felt balls) tumble on the farmlands and drop seeds on their path. As the game proceeds over a number of rounds, the seeds grow into new tumbleweeds.

西部劇でよく見る転がる雑草を育てる、転がる拡大再生産！
ここは西部劇が大流行した世界で需要が急増したのは、タンブルウィードだった！
あなたはタングルウィード農家に転身した。
タングルウィードを繁殖させ、売ってお金を稼ごう！
風を読み、雑草を転がそう！ タンブルウィードは通り道に種を落とす！
助っ人の力を巧みに使い、大金持ちを目指そう！

プレイヤーはタイルを繋げ、農地を広げます。
その上をタンブルウィード（フェルトボール）が転がり、タネを落とします。
タネはラウンドが進むと成長し、新たなタンブルウィードになります。

Be a story.
BEASTORY.

Your are a party of beasts who much travel deep into the Emerald Forest to defeat the unknown.  Can you survive?  If you risk too much by going too far into the forest you can lose your life.  Search for items and upgrade your skills along the way.

This is a cooperative RPG style board game, where players play beast character like a monkey who wields a short sword, a bear with an axe.

In the game there is a :
Preparation phase
Event phase
- In the Event phase there will be either an: Action phase & Chance phase
OR a Battle phase 
Standby phase
Boss phase which occurs on your 5th day in the forest

Enough planning - it's time for the Blue Moon Monster Mixer! Extend the life of the party with three distinct modules that offer new ways to play and earn Spook Juice. The Monster Mixer also contains a few other small modules that can be mixed-and-matched as you please.

With this expansion, it's good to mix things up. Try hosting a dinner party and catering to the demands of your guests. Or maybe enter in a body-building contest as you assemble your very own abomination. You could even uncover clues to prevent a murder and catch a would-be criminal before time runs out!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ROBOTECH: Crisis Point Game – Danger Zone Promo Expansion
description:
A booster pack that adds 6 new expansion cards to the Robotech: Crisis Point game. The cards included are:

Army of the Southern Cross
-new Unit card: VHT-2 Myrmidon
-new Hero card: Bowie Grant
- new Command card: Supporting Fire

The Masters
-new Unit card: Imperial Bioroid
-new Hero card: Clone Masters
-new Command card: Spring the Trap

Includes five new cards for the base game:

- Dentist
- Speedy
- The Oracle
- Unique
- Up To Eleven

Also has a cover card and instructions card, included in the sealed plastic sleeve.

A single promotional card for Cindr from the Game Boy Geek 2020 Kickstarter funding campaign.

Five stars.

Meet up: Elvish Ales
Next step: Serenades
Next level: Enchanted Evening

Upgrade any die by one here.

Bonus: Roll a spark on your last roll of a successful date, and add 1 Love point to your total.

boardgame: Muse: Game Boy Geek Season 8 Kickstarter Promo Card
description:
Available from the Game Boy Geek Season 8 2020 Kickstarter.

New art card to mix in with the base game. Has no special ability.

boardgame: Visitor In Blackwood Grove: Promo Pack
description:
Five card promotional card pack. No instructions included.

The cards have illustrations of:
- a gold ring
- a police box (the T.A.R.D.I.S.?)
- a light saber
- a winged octopus
- two young women

Hanabi: Black Powder is the 2nd expansion for Hanabi. It adds 10 cards to the game to create a special 6th Fireworks display!

Black Powder card are never part of color clues, they must be played from 5 to 1, and there are three 5s and one 1 instead.

It is compatible with the base R&R version of the game (European versions of the game are not compatible with this expansion, unless all cards are used with opaque sleeves).

boardgame: Pretty Little Liars: Untold Secrets Game
description: Everyone has secrets, even you! Players travel the board, answer questions about the show, and unravel secrets about each other! Over 500 questions will keep you guessing, but the most fun is when you look in the secrets box and find and reveal secrets about other players!

Nowadays, the gnomes of Sweetland are a happy and irreverent people, because their ancestors created the Legendary Chocolate Spring, with which they were able to invent the most delicious candies and desserts!

Candy Wars is a board game for 1-4 people, where players control gnomes trying to fulfil their objectives around a 3D map, using special weapon and utility cards to power up their moves and rolling dices to defeat their goblin enemies and get advantage against their fellow gnomes!

Your house is being overrun with zombies. They will come after you one after the other. Defend yourself with whatever is in arm’s reach. It could be a bowling ball or dinner or your own booger. Help each other or play every person for themselves. Defend yourself or be overrun.

Flummox your Zombies takes some strategy that might be difficult for younger players, but adults and players 10+ may help younger players. You may play as teams or as individuals. You may trade weapon cards to help each other or keep the weapons for yourself. Zombie Flummox is a card game with 6 different types of zombie cards and 10 different types of weapon cards.
 Each round a player will draw 1 zombie card and 1 weapon card. Use the weapon cards to kill or delay the advancing zombies. The use of each weapon and which zombie it effects is described on the weapon card. Tracking sheets will be included to help you keep track of the number of rounds until a zombie reaches you.

The zombie cards vary in strengths and the zombies have a certain amount of rounds before they reach you. You are out of the game when they reach you.

—description from the designer

Eight Gods is a quick 2-6 player card game played in taverns and homes throughout the Wandering Sea (i.e. the Sleeping Gods world). Rules are also included for a solitaire game commonly played in the Wandering Sea.

In more detail, Eight Gods can be played by 2 - 6 players in about 20 minutes. Each round, players are dealt a 6 card hand. Then, they give two cards away, play their hand, and score their hand. Scoring also happens in the gift and play phases, so there are many opportunities to score points and various strategies are viable. The game has a variable ending, once enough Gods are asleep. It is a morphed form of cribbage that very loosely tells the story of putting the Gods to sleep.

—description from the designer

Hollywood Sensation is a trick-taking game for two players, each of whom takes on the role of a starlet hoping for fame — but not too much fame, mind you, as that would be gauche, and besides, it would be harder to hide any scandals!

Each round, the players randomize the strength order of the four suits. Cards collected in two suits are worth positive points, and the other two suits are negative points.

Each player is dealt a small hand, then the top card of the deck is flipped face up. The players compete in a must-follow trick-taking game, with the winner of each trick taking the face-up card from the deck in their hand, and the other player doing the same with a face-down card from the top of the deck. After the round, the players' points are converted to a type of meta points by moving a pawn on a red carpet.

In subsequent rounds, the player who is behind in meta points begins the round by setting cards aside from their hand to create a small private deck; they will draw from this deck once the main draw pile is exhausted.

The year is 630 AD, Medieval times. You need to attack other kingdoms and have the kneel before you while defending your own from enemy attacks. Recruit brave knights and assassins to crush your enemies. Get priests and peasants into your kingdom to expand faster. But don't lose your king or your kingdom will crumble before your very eyes.

At the start of the game, each player starts with a king card in their hand. If you lose your king card you are out of the game. You have to play cards to force others to lose their king. Some cards may help you to gain cards and other cards may be used to deplete your opponents cards.

Certain types of cards can be used to battle against other people. You can use Horsemen, Knights, Guards, Squires and even Peasants to attack and defend your kingdom. If you play enough cards, your opponent will have to discard 3 cards. Other players can join in the battle by playing their battle cards during a battle onto either player's side.

Envoy cards like the assassin, thief, imposter, baron and strategist have effects that involve taking a random card from an opponent’s hand. If you choose the right card you get a good effect. Barons and strategists have good effects for both players. assassins, thieves and imposters have negative effects for the other player. Your opponent won't know what card you used until you take one of their cards. But they can chose the guard against you if you want to play and envoy card. By playing guards, they can check cards in your hand for assassins, thieves and imposters. But if they check your barons or strategists, they will have to discard their guards.

You have to convince your opponent not to guard against you in order to play assassins, thieves and imposters or you can trick them into guarding against your barons or strategists to make them lose their guards.

By having power points, you can expand your kingdoms even faster. For every 5 power points you have at the start of your turn, you can draw an extra card. Power points allow you to gain cards faster quickly turning you into a dominating force. Cards like Peasants, Priests, Princesses and Princes will give you power points.

When all the players have lost their king cards the last player left is the winner!

—description from the designer

boardgame: おかしなおちゃかい (Okashina Ochakai)
description:
In おかしなおちゃかい (Okashina Ochakai), players compete in two teams of 2 players each.  It is a trick-taking game in which players are not dealt a hand of cards, but will draw them one at a time from an initial hand.  The cards show the expected suit and rank on the front, and some cards also show a point value (positive or negative.) All cards show their suit on the back, and if a card is worth points, that is indicated with a symbol.

When a player picks up a card to add it to their eventual hand for the round, they can choose to have it face themselves or the other players.  If it faces the other players, the card drawer's team earns a resource which may later allow that team to play cards from each others hands.

Points are scored by the presence of certain cards in tricks and the first team to 30 points wins.

boardgame: World War III: Team Yankee – Soviet
description:
This is the third updated book for World War III: Team Yankee. 
It features all the great options and units that were in Red Thunder, but with the addition of lots more Formations and Units. 
World War III: Team Yankee – Soviet: Soviet expands and enhances the forces available in Red Thunder for the Soviet Army.

What is Different?
There is more choice. More tank options with the introduction of the T-80, T-62M, T-55AM, and more Motor Rifle options with the BMP-3. These add more Formations to choose from with the addition of the T-80 Tank Battalion, T-62M Tank Battalion, and T-55AM Tank Battalion, as well as the elite veteran T-80 Shock Tank Company

There are a bunch more new formation and support Units and vehicles as well. The 2S6 Tunguska AA Platoon, the BMP-3 Recon Platoon, BMP-3 Motor Rifle Company, BMP-3 Shock Recon Platoon, BMP Shock Motor Rifle Company, BM-27 Hurricane Rocket Launcher Battery, and TOS-1 Thermobaric Rocket Launcher Battery.

Don’t worry, all your old favourites are still available. In fact these can also gain access to the new anti-tank, anti-aircraft, motor rifle, and recon options. You can still take a T-64 or T-72 Tank Battalion, or a BMP Motor Rifle Battalion, or an Afgantsy Air Assault Battalion.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: World War III – Team Yankee 2 (Battlefront)

description:
''Artifacts on Track is a conceptual scavenger hunt.

Build a track from interlocking letter tiles. Reveal five artifacts. Roll one die. Move your meeple to a letter that matches any one of those artifacts’ attributes’ first letter. Collect the artifact. For example, [SUN] matches S(un) R(ound) H(ot) Y(ellow) U(niverse). Collect the most artifacts to win the game. Simultaneous game play means no waiting for other players. Fast-paced fun. Conceptual symbol interpretation for all ages.

—description from the designer

boardgame: World War III: Team Yankee – Soviet Starter Force: T-80 Shock Tank Company
description:
The Soviet Union’s most powerful forces are stationed in East Germany and Czechoslovakia, poised on the border with West Germany ready to strike at a moment’s notice. 
They are equipped with the very best Soviet industry has to offer, from sophisticated missile firing T-80 and T-64 tanks, powerful T-72 tanks, and tough, reliable, and upgraded T-62M and T-55AM tanks to versatile BMP infantry fighting vehicles, as well as plentiful BTR-60 armoured personnel carriers. 
Supported by elite Afgansty Air Assault troops, powerful self-propelled artillery there will be little that the corrupt capitalist armies of NATO can do to stop them. 
Soon the western workers will be free and the dawn of a new socialist age will begin!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: World War III – Team Yankee 2 (Battlefront)

boardgame: Victory at Sea: Regia Marina Fleet
description:
At the time of Italy’s entry into World War Two, she possessed a modern and – on paper at least – highly effective fleet. Four battleships and eight heavy cruisers were available, with three more battleships being fitted out. However, there were no aircraft carriers (initially), not least because the Regia Marina was intended to operate near to friendly air bases in Italy and Africa.

As might be expected from a force operating among the islands of the Mediterranean, light forces were quite numerous, including 14 light cruisers, 128 destroyers and 62 motor torpedo boats, which was a weapon favoured by the Italians and well suited to local conditions. No less than 115 submarines were available. The main Italian naval base was at Taranto, home of the battleship force. Lighter groups were based out of ports on the Italian mainland, Sicily and the Red Sea.

The Regia Marina was primarily tasked with interrupting British logistics and trade through the Mediterranean, and with keeping the Axis nations’ links to North Africa open. Major actions with the Royal Navy were not desirable nor really necessary for this mission to be carried out.

Contents:
1 Aquila-class Carrier - Aquila
1 Conte di Cavour-class Battleship - Conte di Cavour 1940
1 Etna-class cruiser - Etna 1942
1 Zara-class cruiser - Pola 1940
1 Luigi Cadorna-class cruiser - Luigi Cadorna 1940
3 Navigatori-class Destroyers
4 Torpedo-Bomber Aircraft - Reggiane RE.2001 Falco II
Ship Cards & Damage Sliders
Assembly Instructions

—description from the publisher

The Littorio class was the first new Italian battleship class for nearly a decade when design work began in 1930. Initially designed to remain within the 35,000-ton Washington Treaty limit, the final displacement was just over 40,000 tons. As well as being good-looking ships, the Littorio-class included a number of new features including high-velocity guns. They were probably the first of the ‘fast battleships’ that would come to dominate capital ship design in the late 1930s and onward.

Vittorio Veneto saw extensive service in World War Two, participating in the Battles of Cape Spartivento in November 1940 and Cape Matapan in March 1941. Though struck by a torpedo at this latter engagement and again later courtesy of a British submarine, she would escape unscathed from the British raid on Taranto of November 1940. 1941 and early 1942 was spent attempting to attack British convoys en route to Malta, until fuel shortages forced the end of such activity. In the armistice of 1943, she was surrendered to the Allies after Italy withdrew from the war, spending the next three years under British control in Egypt. Thereafter she was allocated to Britain as a war prize and was scrapped.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Victory at Sea: Regia Marina Submarines & MTB sections
description:
Submarines: Compared to a German Type VII C submarine, the Marcello-class were much larger, displacing 1,060 tons versus 769. Speed and range between the two classes were almost similar, but the Marcello-class had more torpedo tubes than the famous U-Boat. The Marcello-class should be considered one of the most successful produced by the Italian shipyards and showed very good qualities – being fast, structurally robust and relatively manoeuvrable.

MTBs: Capable of 45 knots, the Italian Motoscafo Armato Silurante (Armed Torpedo Boat) or MAS Boats were extremely active in the Mediterranean theatre. Though they were not well suited to rougher seas, they still achieved a great deal of note, including the torpedoing of HMS Capetown and an attack on the harbour of Malta, though the latter was a failure that cost two MAS Boats.

Based on German S1 class S-Boats the Italians found in the port of Cattaro in Yugoslavia, the MS Boat was larger and far more seaworthy than the MAS designs. Though not as fast, it was far better suited to long range patrols in the Mediterranean being more stable and more comfortable. It was an MS Boat that sank the largest vessel of any MTB in the war, HMS Manchester, in 1942.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Unity: Battlefield Medics of Unified Space for Renegade Scout 2nd Edition
description:
The third supplement for Renegade Scout 2nd edition focuses on battlefield medical personnel, allowing you to both add a specialist medic to your squads, as well as employ medical personalities.

As always, a bit of background information is provided as well as new army list entries and two new Skills.

The "..of Unified Space" series is intending to be a series of small, bite-sized expansions for Renegade Scout 2nd edition', adding new content and options to the game system, expanding the universe and generally increasing the amount of fun to be had.

-description from the publisher's website

In ユリゲラ (Uri Geller), player compete in a world tournament to demonstrate their supernatural powers by bending spoons with their psychic abilities. Aim for the championship, while being careful not to bend the spoon too much and be suspected of being nothing more than a magician!

The Monopoly board game meets South Africa's national rugby union team in this edition of Springbok Monopoly.
The Springboks are one of the most feared and admired rugby teams.

The Springbok Monopoly board game is a twist on the classic board game. Participants travel around the board and buy their favoritism Springbok players and rugby stadiums – they then negotiate with opponents in an attempt to build a rugby empire. A rugby ball, boot, goal posts, whistle and trophy replace the original tokens like the top hat. Monopolists will build stands and stadiums and trade their way to success.

Comrades! Disaster has struck the city of Meeplersk! REACTOR No.4 at the Boris Yeltzin Nuclear Power Facility has exploded. A tumultuous fire has started spreading radioactive material into the air and the explosion has compromised the dam at the water cooling reservoir. The situation is grave and the Soviet high command is looking for someone to blame. We must rescue our loyal comrades and prove to the Premier we should not be the one sentenced to the Gulag!

In Reactor No.4 players take turns to mitigate disasters occurring as a result of an explosion in a Nuclear Power Plant.  Each round the map is modified by wind blowing fires and nuclear fallout across a map as well as new fires and floods breaking out.

The players then take two of the following actions in an attempt to score points:

     Move up to 2 adjacent locations
     Evacuate survivors (score 1 VP per survivor)
     Extinguish fires (score 1 VP per fire)
     Pump out flood water (score 1 VP per flood)
     Clean up radiation (score 1 VP per radiation)


The available actions change each round and some locations provide modifications to scoring, or allow additional actions to be taken.

At the end of each round the level of disappointment felt by the Soviet High Command is modified based on the number of deaths as well as unresolved disasters on prized locations.

Once the disappointment level reaches 20 the game ends and the lowest scoring player is sent to the Gulag!

—description from the designer

USA-Xpert is a combined alignment and guessing game with collector cards to better know each state of the USA. The principle is simple. From a card which shows a flag, you have first to guess either its capital or main city. Then you place the card in a line, ranking by area or population (your choice). If you loose, you get more cards to get rid off. With USA-Xpert, learn all states' capital, largest city, motto, nickname, abbreviation, great seal and flag and quiz your family and friends.

—description from the designer

In Savannah Territory, you play as a ranger who keeps the peace in the savannah by dividing the territory to prevent conflicts between the animals.

However, the savannah is a world of the weak and the strong, and the rangers will not be able to avoid kicking each other down to the ground. You need to place the animals well while interfering with other players.

Once the game board is made up of curiously shaped savannah boards, each player's assigned animal tip is placed in a way that prevents the same type of animal from being placed next to each other. If you can place all the animal tips or prevent other players from placing any more, you will be awarded a medal.

Where can you put them to disturb other players? Which animal tip should I have left at the end of the day to help me survive? It is a simple game, but you can't let your guard down until the end.

The ease with which this game can be played from around 8 years old, and the depth of the trouble that comes with understanding the theories, make it an addictive game.

—description from the designer
publishers: Banso Inc. (株式会社バンソウ)

description:
It's feeding time in the monster canteen!

In this fast-paced logic game, only the one who gets fed up with all the monsters wins - faster than the other players. That sounds easier than it is. Because every monster has its own preferences - and intolerances!

Every turn each player reveals the top card of their pile and puts it in the middle of the table. Now they have to think fast and put their plate on the card that shows the monster or item that will be left over after everybody has been eating. To decide on who will be left over there are 5 eating rules the players have to know. Who can keep track of everything and tell who will be left over? Often the strongest monster consumes the weaker ones, but sometimes even a hungry umbrella will use its chance to consume a monster...

The player that was the fastest to choose the right monster or food wins the round and collects the revealed cards as victory points. The game ends when you played through the whole card deck.

-description from designer

The Four Tribes: Upgrade Kit allows those who own the original Game Crafter version of Four Tribes to upgrade to the Library Edition of the game. This includes the following:


     A redesigned box that is twice as big, much sturdier than the original and skinned in new artwork
     A 16 page full-color instruction booklet replacing the original folded instruction sheet
     Adds 6 all-new winter village cards that add about 5-10 minutes to game time
     Adds the Fire mini expansion (6 new cards)
     Adds the Flood mini expansion (6 new cards)
     Adds 2 additional Long House cards (one for each player to house influenced village elders)



NOTE: This kit upgrades the Four Tribes (English second edition) to the Four Tribes: Library Edition.

It is a system that simulates the fighting between galleys in the 16th-17th centuries and which pitted various Christian powers against the Ottoman Empire and its tributaries of the Maghreb.

This game was included in the Alea 35 magazine Italia 44.

Fun and simple game, designed for two or more players, to easily represent these exciting naval battles.

Age: 12+
Player: 2-4
Time: 60+
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Clash of Deck is an evolving do-it-yourself card game. This is the first expansion of the game. Each month, a new standalone expansion of the game is released, which allows you to create new decks and thus increase the replayability of the game.

In Clash of Deck, you play as a Sorcerer Lord whose objective is to take control of the safest islands of the Erret Archipelago. To do this you will have to fight other Witchlords.

This expansion is made up of 16 new cards as well as 2 new visuals of Bridge cards. New types of cards are appearing: Spells that have immediate effects and Buildings that remain in play. You will also be able to discover 6 new special abilities that will add even more combos and depth to your games: Evasion, Defender, Untouchable, Gust, Focus and Demolitionist.

—description from the publisher

Challenge yourself or work with friends to solve an endless supply of self-generating puzzles! A terrific way to break from your screen and improve your spatial reasoning skills.

The game is played with six blinks, each time you reset the game of Puzzle 101, the blinks will randomly generate you a puzzle by changing the colors of sections of the blinks. Then you need to assemble the blinks attaching like colors together to solve the puzzle.

Magic Creatures is a card game designed for up to 7 players, age 6 and older.  The deck consists of 70 cards.  20 of these cards are “Magic Cards”.  The other 50 cards are gameplay cards.

The magic cards either have a creature and value on one side that gives the card bearer a Skeleton with a “Damage Boost” point value, or a Ghost with a “Health Boost” point value. The gameplay cards consist of five distinct “suits”, with ten cards in each suit.  Each card within each suit displays the suit type, signified by a “Magic Creature” and a point value for “Damage”, as well as a point value for “Health”.  The point values for damage and health vary from one card to the next, even within the same suit.

The Magic Cards are shuffled and placed face down in a stack on the table.  The gameplay cards are shuffled and dealt to the players.  Each player is dealt one card at a time, face down, for a maximum of ten cards per player.  The youngest player goes first and gameplay continues rotating from one player to the next in a clockwise direction.

Players take turns using the gameplay cards in their hands and the Magic Cards on the table to "attack" other players. The damage and health values on the cards are used to determine the winner. The loser of an attempted attack loses his/her gameplay card. The last player with gameplay cards remaining in their hand wins the game.

—description from the designer

OVERVIEWThe lunar Mare, plural Maria, are large, flat and dark basaltic plains of lower elevation on the Earth's Moon formed by ancient volcanic eruptions. The term Mare, which in Latin means "sea" was erroneously applied to such features by telescopic observers of the 17th century who mistook them for actual seas. In actuality, Maria are huge basins containing lava flows marked by craters, ridges, faults, straight and meandering valleys called rilles and are devoid of water. Maria are the largest topographic, dark and relatively young features on the Moon. Together with the bright, heavily cratered and very old lunar Highlands (Latin: Terrae), they form the landscape of the Moon. Maria are less reflective than the Terrae and can be seen from Earth with the naked eye. Back in the days, the Apollo program, led by NASA, brought back many samples of lunar rock and soil material. It proved that among other minerals and elements, Maria are to be rich in Iron, Titanium, Magnesium and Silicon. Even three new minerals were discovered on the Moon and named Armalcolite, Tranquillityite and Pyroxferroite. Since then, besides being a research object, the Moon becomes increasingly, a point of interest to many of Earth's public and private companies, which perceive it as their next source for acquiring and extracting resources in the future ...

... in Luna Mare: Mineralis & Dominatio game, players compete in collecting elements discovered on the surface of the Moon with aim to discover additional, valuable, new resources and minerals. In addition to gathering resources they also fight for control over Maria to establish a rewarding Moon dominance.

Gameplay introduces and smoothly combines 2 core mechanics of pick up & deliver and area control in a single game. Together with light combat, score rivalry, units and cards management or team play, it provides a deep, tactical experience.

2 to 3 players may compete with each other in multiplayer games. Also, 4 players may play the game by being divided into 2 teams of 2 players. Playing cooperatively team members compete with the opposite team.

The game is based on a strong, realistic theme. Included map represents quite real boundaries of the lunar Maria seen from the Earth, as well as the Apollo landing positions. Also elements and minerals with their names, amount and composition are based on real discoveries, research and available publications.

				
				
					TWO GAMES IN ONELuna Mare: Mineralis & Dominatio is a single board game that consists of 2 separate gameplay modes and experiences - Mineralis and Dominatio.

Although sharing the same goal concept, each gameplay mode offers different mechanics ratios and ways of playing the game, forcing players to adapt their skills and tactics. Mineralis focuses more on a pick up & deliver mechanics with a support of an area control. While Dominatio strongly prefers an area control with a complementary assistance of a pick up & deliver.

Besides using the same, basic gameplay mechanics and components, both modes also introduce their own, unique features. Mineralis has its element delivery and new resource discoveries. Dominatio, on the other hand, provides a constant fight for domination over the most valuable regions and elements.

Each mode comes with a dedicated map, rulebook and a few additional, individual components. They may be easily treated as a standalone, full-featured games.

Choose the way of playing and dominate the resources and surface of the Moon!


				
				
					Check the forum thread for game presentation and all official resources/files/links
[OFFICIAL] - Game presentation + PnP files + Links + WIP Thread + Digital versions
				
				
					2021 Two Player Print and Play Game Design Contest - achievements
 
     BEST GAME: 1st place!
     Best gamer's game: 1st place!
     Best game to play remotely: 1st place!
     Best mechanics: 1st place!
     Best rule book: 5th place!
     Best art/graphic design: 1st place!
     Best use of theme: 1st place!

boardgame: O Homem Que Mordeu o Cão: Escândalos
description:
The game is based on the invention and narration of strange and bizarre stories, from the cards that come out, corresponding to the questions: “Where?”, “Who?”, “What?”, “To Who?”, “With What? it's because?".

In this expansion “O Homem que Mordeu o Cão: Escândalos”, players set out in search of even more bizarre and scandalous stories to tell, at the right time, and make their listeners laugh. 
There are many possibilities! Put creativity to the test.

This game can be played on the main game board or individually.

Using as an expansion, the rules of the board game will be used.
Using only this box, our board will be replaced by two special dice.
The "Action" dice will be rolled and we will do whatever it takes on the face of that dice. "Star", forces us to tell the story in front of us, steal letters from another player, be punished by the other players in our next narration, or maybe change the theme and then we launch the second dice to see which theme will be changed.

We have time counted by the hourglass and if we get away with it and manage to convince the other players, we earn a prestigious coin.
At the end of the game, the player with the most prestigious coins wins.

Mord bei Tisch is a criminal dinner game in which 6-8 players can participate to find out who among them is the murderer of the found victim. In addition to that, every player gets a personal task to fulfil during the evening, which can include finding certain information via asking, hiding certain information during the game, convince other players to join them in certain acts or act in a very specific way during the game.

The Berghotel case is about the murder on the charity event manager Maria Bloom in an isolated hotel in the Swiss mountains!

Mord bei Tisch is a criminal dinner game in which 6-8 players can participate to find out who among them is the murderer of the found victim. In addition to that, every player gets a personal task to fulfil during the evening, which can include finding certain information via asking, hiding certain information during the game, convince other players to join them in certain acts or act in a very specific way during the game.

The Zirkus case is about the murder on Freddy, the tamer of circus Martini. Circus Martini is run in the eighth generation, but spectators become less and less. On top of that, tonight there was a fire and Freddy was found stabbed. It is up to you to find his murderer!

boardgame: Civilizations Collide: The Wars of the Aztecs, the Inca, the Maya, and the Conquistadores – A Supplement for Feudal Patrol
description:
Civilizations Collide supplement roughly covers the period of the Spanish Conquest of the 16th Century.

This supplement is designed to allow the players to recreate battles both during the Conquest –and in the centuries before it happened.

Optional and special rules are covered first, then weapons, figure and unit characteristics, and a couple of suggested scenarios.

- description from rules supplement

You and your crew are on a mission manning an experimental ship to prove that alternate realities exist. The ship malfunctions and crashes on an unknown planet in an unknown galaxy in an unknown reality full of ferocious monsters.
 Each player takes control of a hero trying to defend against an endless swarm of monsters, all while repairing the downed ship. Players each roll 5 dice at the start of every round that can be spent to perform actions such as moving, searching, gaining XP, and attacking. They also have a unique starting ability that changes gameplay for that player from the very beginning.

The face of a die only affects searching and attacking, though there are many ways to manipulate the dice keeping randomness at a minimum. There are also several free actions that do not require spending dice, giving players a large variety of choice and wildly-varying turns.

Along with the 6 unique heroes to choose from, each hero also has their own 6-card deck of Level Up cards. Only 3 of these cards are used each game, providing a unique experience even when playing as the same hero multiple times.

Monsters spawn onto the board and are trying to get to the ship to destroy it once and for all. Monsters are on cards which have die faces on them acting as their HP. To attack these creatures, players spend the matching dice to damage or defeat the monster. At the beginning of the game, the monsters only have 2 HP. However, as the game progresses the monsters get increasingly difficult with more HP for each beast. Players may want to gain XP and level up their heroes to combat these stronger baddies.

The monsters are attracted to the smell of leaking oil coming from the ship. To prevent more monsters from spawning, players will want to contribute scrap metal to the middle of the board each round to plug the leak. Although, while searching for more metal, heroes may uncover a boss monster who is the fiercest of them all.

The ship has 4 damaged rooms that must be repaired. Players will find ship parts for each room by searching different locations. Once a ship part is brought back to the ship, players choose what section of the matching room they wish to repair, providing a one-time ability and giving more freedom of choice. Each room also an alternative side of one-time abilities which can be chosen or selected at random during setup, offering more replayability.

Once the heroes have repaired the ship, cleared the area of monsters, and everyone is on board, they can take-off and return home. Dice manipulation, as well as most actions not requiring specific dice, leaves players with the difficult decision of whether to attack, search, repair, gain XP, etc. rather than focusing on getting the best roll. The many different strategies to victory, along with the unique heroes and setup, provides a one-of-a-kind experience with each play.

It was all going so well… and then monsters showed up.

-description from designer

boardgame: もっとも桃太郎なのはもちろん俺！(Mottomo Momotaro nanowa Mochiron Ore)
description:
"Mottomo Momotarō nanowa Mochiron Ore!" translates roughly to “Make no Mistake, I’m the Most Momotaro!”.
 
Momotarō is a famous hero from Japanese legend but recently there’s been an influx in Momotarō imposters.

  In order to prove he is the real Momotarō, Momotarō needs to gather up his old traveling buddies. Just who really is the genuine Momotarō?   

A light set collection game featuring 14 cute characters and easy-to-grasp rules. The first player to gather dog, monkey and pheasant wins the game!

There are two types of cards: items and friends. Each card has a Momotarō-ness score in the top left corner, as well an effect.

During each turn of the game, a player may chose to either (A) draw & play a card, (B) use a Kibidango chip, or (C) pass.

 If the player chooses A:
 After drawing a card from the stack they will proceed to place one card face up from his or her hand. This card will belong to the player unless another player takes it using (B). In addition, the effect of the card can be used.

  If the player chooses B: 
The player should declare their use of the Kibidango chip and put the chip into the disposal pile. He or she can then chose one friend card from a single opponent’s exposed cards. In addition, the effect of the stolen card may be used.  

Note: You may not choose action B if the player in front of you has just used a chip on his or her turn. Chips also cannot be used if the card stack is empty.  

If the player chooses C:
 Nothing happens.

  The first player to collect the friend cards of dog, monkey, and pheasant wins. Alternatively, there is also a wild card that can be used to replace one of these three animals to win.

  When all players have empty hands and no one has been able to collect dog, monkey, and pheasant, use the total Momotarō-ness score of all the cards that belong to a player (placed in front of them) to determine the winner. If there is a tie, the player with the most friend cards wins. If there is still a tie, both players are the winners.

boardgame: Space Cats Fight Fascism: Secret Meowssions
description: Secret Meowssions is an expansion that adds new elements to the base game of Space Cats Fight Fascism, for those who want to extend their gameplay and strategize in new ways. In this expansion, cats will have additional missions that they have to complete, without the fascists finding out what you are up to! These meowssions will include freeing captured animals of the resistance, seizing a fascist space station, distributing food to hungry animals, and more. If the cats are able to complete these meowssions, they’ll get major rewards, such as gaining the support of local animals, new tools to fight the fascists, extra actions on your turn, and more. But if the fascists uncover your plans, there will be serious consequences...

In this game of cards and cubes, players assume the role of entrepreneurs in the far future investing in mineral mines on the planet Mars. Various types of ore are represented by colored cubes contained in a small cloth bag called "the mine." A large card represents the Ore Train with a set of ore  cubes available to all players, but players may choose to draw a random cube from the mine instead. Each player has a large card representing their own refinery which includes conveyors to process the five types of ore. Each player also has a small screen called their Warehouse, behind which they will conceal their store of refined minerals as the game goes on. There are also cards which can be drawn by the players to aid their efforts, or hinder the efforts of their competitors. On each turn, a player may draw a card (if they wish), and will select an ore cube to add to their refinery. When ore completes the refining process, the player is rewarded by special tasks or choices they can make, including playing their cards. The end game is triggered when one player has refined five "rarium" cubes (the most valuable of the Martian minerals). Then the winner is determined by the greatest number of refined minerals in the player's warehouse.

—description from the publisher

Presidential Rummy is a parody card game for 1-4 players where you match and swap "Presidents" based on their different attributes.

Be the first to score 100 points by achieving goals and you win! But watch out for the Drain the Swamp cards...

Features parody names and fun art evoking the Presidents of the United States.

The game that aims to be a fun game between players of different ages. Through questions, answers, challenges and competitions, players will learn a little more about the universe of other generations. In the busy and ultra connected world in which we live, Batalha das Gerações provides unique moments of family life. What is Kpop? What does LP mean? What is flopping? These and other doubts will be resolved in this game by different generations.

There are 360 ​​cards with questions, answers and challenges

Name three national holidays. Or three famous “Silvas”. It's easy, isn't it?
Now imagine doing it in a few seconds, and with all the players watching you. You will be surprised, as it is not as easy as it looks!
Contra o Tempo brings 300 cards with funny and unusual situations for you to quote. A timer marks the time you will have to give the answers. And time is very short!
The game even has cards at different levels of difficulty, for you to play with your family or friends.
Try playing a game Contra o Tempo and see that not everything is easy when you are under pressure!

Worship the gods, prepare a coffin, and die well.

For the Ancient Egyptians, death is something not even the Gods can evade. It is fated and, at the same time, it is the beginning of a new life.

No one knows when death deems to visit, therefore as Egyptian nobility you must prepare the necessary burial accessories and make the proper arrangements for the world that awaits you after this one.

Only the player who readies a coffin of the highest value during his or her lifetime will have the privilege to share a destiny with the Great Osiris and experience a blissful eternal life.

A card game that uses simultaneous action selection and drafting mechanics. Choose gods who will favor you with various blessings and decide whether to bury treasure in your own coffin or choose to steal treasure from others. The player who collects the most treasure wins the game.

When playing with the “Short Rules” which require the usage of fewer cards, the game can be casually played in around 30 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Fates and Favors is a strategic, team-based, track-movement game combined with Greek mythology.

Players are Greek heroes invited to Mount Olympus to pay tribute to the gods by building temples in their honor. To win the game, heroes work with their teammate to complete two temples before the other team.

Heroes use the game’s dynamic card play to strategically choose actions in response to the evolving gameplay. Fate cards are the primary basis for movement while Favor cards are powerful interventions from the Olympians. While heroes have the means to control their own fate through clever moves and efficient teamwork, a few perfectly timed godly favors can unexpectedly shift the momentum of the competition in the blink of an eye!

Fates and Favors’ is finely-tuned to provide heroes memorable experiences with epic comebacks, sweet revenge, and high-stakes anticipation as both teams race to complete their temples!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Be The Greatest Soccer Board Game
description:
You start as a raw talent, a soccer player of unspecified club, gender, race or nation.

Live the footballer's lifestyle and build form by winning matches, gain ability on the training pitch, accumulate wealth and seek fame in the media.

Get help from in-game friends, train hard and stay injury free, meet the press, use tactics to win matches and take some time out to relax before advancing your career.

Use your gravitas to gain promotion to higher reputation levels and be a star player.

Become the G.O.A.T. The Greatest Of All Time.'

—description from the designer

Manen Tur-Retur is a large Swedish space race game to the moon and back. The winner is the first to return to Earth.

Playing pieces move by roll of a dice and proceed first to a space station before reaching the moon. Players trek across the moon surface before returning to Earth via the Space station.

Along the path players can take detours, be delayed or struck by meteors.

—user summary

FC De Kampioenen: the most fun and popular football team in Flanders now also has its own MONOPOLY edition.

Discover the iconic locations from the series. Buy Fernand's dovecote or Carmen's newsagents shop. And who knows, you might become the new owner of the soccer field of De Kampioenen.

Another window broken? "Zal ‘t gaan, ja??" That's a 100M fine.
Do you win the dog show with Nero? Tournée général! You pay each other player 50M.

The unpredictable world of Push Up Poppies offers rewards and inflicts pain. Plantation owners in Afghanistan have devilish tricks and creative tools to grow and harvest their poppies.

In this card game, each player has their own poppy plantation and their goal is to harvest the most poppies by maximizing their plantation's productivity and stifling the opposition.

There are four types of card:

     The Field cards, which are the cards the players can harvest. Most of these are poppy cards, which have a value from 1 to 8.
     The Infrastructure cards, which permanently affect the player's plantation in various ways.
     The Action cards, which boost poppy production or sabotage the competition. These cards are discarded immediately after being played.
     The Wild cards, which behave differently depending on what the player chooses them to be.


Each player must have five cards in their hand at the start of their turn. During their turn, the player must draw one card from the common stack and then play one card, either by placing it in front of them in their plantation (Field or Infrastructure cards) or by resolving its action (Action cards). They can also simply discard a card. If they so choose, they can play a second one and if they do, they have to draw a second card from the stack afterwards.

As soon as a player has five Field cards in their plantation, they must harvest it. The harvest is worth the combined value of the poppy cards harvested. The first player reaching 69 points wins!

Note: One deck allows to play with up to six players, but one can combine several decks together to play with many more people.

パズールレーン (Pazur Lane) is a card game from Japan based on fanfic of the mobile game Azure Lane.

The players compete to become the best commander. Play Warship cards from your hand to add team members and perform various actions.

Line up the cards you play in front of you in the order of playing them. The cards of the same color (faction) or number will be combined when placed adjacent to each other. When the 3 cards are combined, the 2 cards on top are dispatched. Contribute to the battle in this way by sending your ships into the battlefield.

At the end of the game, the players receive medals based on the ranking of the different color forces dispatched into the battlefields. The player who collects the most medals wins.

※アズールレーン（スマホゲーム）の二次創作ゲームです。
最高の指揮官を目指して競いましょう。
艦(カード)を手札から自分の前に出すことで新たな仲間を増やしたり、特定のアクションを実行できます。自分の前に出したカードは使った順に並べていきますが、色(陣営)か数字が同じカードは隣接すると合体します。３枚合体すると上の２枚が出撃します。こうして戦場に艦を送り込むことで戦いに貢献しましょう。ゲーム終了時、色別で見て、戦場に送り込んだ戦力の順位に応じた勲章を受け取ります。最も勲章を集めたプレイヤーが勝利します。
publisher's summary
artists: Sasari Hinoe (ササリ ヒノエ)

description:
Description from Washington Post, January 21, 1982:

My mother's sister is my aunt--20 points; my autn's neice is me--30 points; but then again, she could be my sister or my cousin, and my grandmother's cousin is my . . . what?

Weaving through the genealogy of who begat whom on the family tree has become a new card game called "Family Treedition," created by Mildred Davidson Austin Smith, an active community volunteer and former opera singer from Northwest.

Smith said she designed the game as a "way to get back in touch with the family," and as an inexpensive form of family entertainment.

The object of the genealogy game is to earn points by placing on a board three cards from your hand that represent a correct relationship between blood relatives. For example, "my father's first cousin is my second cousin."

The deck's 64 cards represent everyone from great-grandmother to third cousin. Of course, there are a few wild cards, to get you out of tight spots. The figures on the cards have what Smith calls Egyptian-like, angular features. These, she said, are universal faces.
Smith said she started working on the game in 1975, "even before Roots came on television." It seemed simple, she said, until she realized there are hundreds of possible combinations of relatives.

Smith obtained a patent on "Family Treedition" two years ago. She paid a printer to produce 100 copies of the game last October and has sold them all, many as Christmas gifts. Another 500 copies are on the way for $14.95 each. The game can be purchased from Smith, or through Stanley 5 & 10 Cent Stores in Brookland.

boardgame: Ze Horror Attack: Le retour de la revanche des héros londoniens! – Vol.1 Cauchemars victoriens
description:
The streets of London are not very safe tonight. A night war is brewing. Will Arsène Lupine, Sherlock Holmes or Van Helsing overcome the Mummy, Frankenstein's creature or even Count Dracula?

Join your faction, recover and play special skills to bring light ... or darkness to London!

Each player randomly receives a faction card that they keep hidden. They also receive three cards, which they can, during their turn, either play or draw (within the limit of three cards in hand).

If he plays a card he keeps it in front of himself and applies its effect.

The winner is the one who scores the most points at the end of the game, the hero cards causing the heroes to score points and lose to the villains and vice versa.

—description from publisher

Needs at least one reading player


“Storytailors. New adventures await!” is an expansion to the “Storytailors” board game where Wilfried and Marie invite you to once again immerse yourselves into the fairytale atmosphere of the everchanging stories and even take part in their creation!

In the expansion you will find 30 new character cards and 6 new stories including 2 exciting tricky stories. And to make the game even more thrilling we added 7 empty double-sided story cards into the box for your very own unique stories — let your imagination go wild and take flight!


     Please note that you need the “Storytailors” base game to enjoy this expansion.

Captain, that ship is huge!
Steady on! While the Klingon B10 Invincible is huge, we have enough ships to bring her down.

The most powerful ships in the galaxy have been unleashed and are now free to rain havoc upon their enemies.

The Klingons have set loose the B10 battleship Invincible upon Federation colonies. The Invincible is supported by C5 light dreadnoughts and C10 heavy super-dreadnoughts. The Federation has sent in its fleet with the battleship USS Mars at the head. It is also supported by dreadnoughts. These titans of war will clash, and who knows what will happen when they do.

There are over 60 starships, adding battleships, dreadnoughts, transports, and tugs to your fleets. There are also nine starbases to add more defenses to your systems. These are for the Federation, Gorn, Hydran, Klingon, Kzinti, Lyran, Romulan, Seltorian, and Tholian empires. The WYNs are not neglected as they get their light tactical transport with and without cargo pods.

This book is an expansion. You must have Klingon Armada Unity or Romulan Armada Unity. Play will be enhanced with the Starmada Unity Rulebook.

This edition unifies the previous Nova and Admiralty editions, so there will no longer be Starmada fans who cannot game together. The new rules preserve the best of both previous systems and add dynamic seeking weapons rules that do what seeking weapons are supposed to do: force the enemy to move out of key areas or shoot his way through a swarm of them.

—description from the publisher

The Klingons are coming and they are loaded for bear!

A meeting of empires as a famous game system joins the Star Fleet Universe. Klingon Armada was done as a joint venture between Amarillo Design Bureau and Majestic Twelve Games. This 86-page rulebook includes the Essential Starmada Rules, 46 starships (Klingon, Federation, Tholian, Orion, Kzinti), and complete rules to bring the Star Fleet Universe alive in the Starmada game system.

This fast-playing game easily handles squadrons of ships as they encounter their enemies and battle to see who is indeed the best commander.

Ships included are the Federation dreadnought, battlecruiser, heavy cruiser, old light cruiser, command cruiser, and frigate; the Klingon C8 dreadnought, C7 heavy battlecruiser, D7 battlecruiser, D6 heavy cruiser, D5 war cruiser, F5 frigate, and D7C command cruiser; Kzinti dreadnought, battlecruiser, and frigate; Tholian patrol corvette, Orion Raider, and many other ships. (There are a total of 46 ships.)

This book is complete and ready to play.

This edition unifies the previous Nova and Admiralty editions, so there will no longer be Starmada fans who cannot game together. The new rules preserve the best of both previous systems, and add dynamic seeking weapons rules that do what seeking weapons are supposed to do: force the enemy to move out of key areas or shoot his way through a swarm of drones.

description:
You stand in an empty field and survey the land around. “This will be Mini Town!” you announce to the birds dramatically. As self-elected mayor, it’s your job to oversee development of the new town. Build your infrastructure and services to best serve the community and, of course, to gain the most points!

Mini Town is a family game for any number of players that plays in around 20 minutes.

Play is simultaneous: each player plays at the same time on their own scoresheet. Each player chooses how to use the rolls of two dice. Other players may make other decisions. One die shows the location (row or column) for the construction. Choose from any of the available squares in the row and column. The other die shows the type of construction (road, park, house, library cafe or shop) that can be built in one square. Each player draws their chosen construction type within their chosen location. When all players have used the dice roll, re-roll for the next turn.

The pad comes with 50 scoresheets.

-description from designer

Capitalist is a deck-building game focused on decision-making, risk, and opportunities. The players get to be rising entrepreneurs. Players start with nothing but the work they can provide. They complete contracts, gain skills from jobs, create impactful connections, invest in the stock market and other investments to gain currency, build and upgrade unique business to complete more difficult tasks, and compete to complete grand achievements such as going to Mars. Capitalist is a race to achievement; the first player to reach the necessary achievement points wins the game.

—description from the designer

The aim of the game is not only to play the cards cleverly, but also to bluff so that the bars bend. Whoever can lay down all his cards first, wins.

—user summary
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
The aim of the game is to be the first to discard all cards. For this purpose, each player whose turn it is may play one or more matching cards, i.e. discard a card that comes next in the sequence of numbers on one or more of the 4 discard piles: on a 3, the 4, etc. After the 13 comes again the one, etc.

—user summary
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
Uncloak and Strike from the Shadows!

A meeting of empires as a famous game system joins the Star Fleet Universe. Romulan Armada was done as a joint venture between Amarillo Design Bureau and Majestic Twelve Games. This 84-page rulebook includes the Essential Starmada Rules, 37 starships (Romulan, Federation, and Gorn) and eight bases (Romulan, Gorn, Federation, Klingon, Kzinti, and Tholian), and complete rules to bring the Star Fleet Universe alive in the Starmada game system.

This fast-playing game easily handles squadrons of ships as they encounter their enemies and battle to see who is indeed the best commander.

Ships included are the Federation battlecruiser, Concordat-class dreadnought, and strike cruiser; the Romulan dreadnoughts (the Behemoth and Condor classes), heavy cruisers (FastHawk, FireHawk, and Kestrel classes), and command cruisers (Conqueror, NovaHawk, and RoyalHawk classes); Gorn heavy battlecruiser, command cruiser, and medium cruiser; battle stations for the Gorns and Romulans, mobile bases for the Federation, Gorns, Klingons, Kzintis, Romulans, and Tholians; an armed priority transport;  and many other ships.

This book is complete and ready to play.

This edition unifies the previous Nova and Admiralty editions, so there will no longer be Starmada fans who cannot game together. The new rules preserve the best of both previous systems, and add dynamic seeking weapons rules that do what seeking weapons are supposed to do: force the enemy to move out of key areas or shoot his way through a swarm of drones.

—description from the publisher

IntroductionPartisans is a drawless game for two players: White and Black. It is played on the spaces (squares) of a 10x10 square board. At the start of the game, white partisans are placed on the a4, g1, d10 and j7 squares, and black partisans are placed on a7, g10, d1 and j4. Additionally, each player must have access to a sufficient number of stones of their own color outside the board.

				
				
					PlayWhite plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, move a partisan of your color to an empty square in a straight orthogonal or diagonal line, without jumping over enemy pieces.

Then, place a stone on an empty square that is a straight orthogonal or diagonal line away from the square onto which the partisan was moved, with no enemy pieces in between along that line. This is called shooting.

Note that partisans can move and shoot over friendly partisans and stones as well as over empty squares.

To compensate for the advantage of moving first, White is not allowed to shoot on their first turn, but they must still move a partisan.

If you have no moves available on your turn, you lose.

				
				
					NotesPartisans is based on Amazons, a game designed by Walter Zamkauskas in 1992.

Dangerous New Enemies on Scanners!

New factions meet as Starmada joins the Star Fleet Universe. Alien Armada was done as a joint venture between Amarillo Design Bureau and Majestic Twelve Games. This 84-page rulebook includes the Tholians (with their deadly webs), the Neo-Tholians (you thought the Tholians were bad?), the Seltorians (the faction that threw the Tholians out of the M81 Galaxy and now they are here on a holy crusade of extermination of the Tholians), and many more ships for the Orion Pirates.

There are over 40 starships including the Tholian dreadnought, war cruiser, web tender, command cruiser, and heavy webcruiser; the Neo-Tholian dreadnought, heavy cruiser, light cruiser, destroyer, and frigate; the Seltorian dreadnought, heavy battlecruiser, heavy cruiser, destroyer, frigate, new heavy cruiser, and light dreadnought; the Orion Battle Raider, war destroyer, battlecruiser, heavy battlecruiser, Double Raider, Slaver, Free Traitor, heavy cruiser, dreadnought, and OK6; police ships for the Gorns, Romulans, and Kzintis; and many other ships.

But it isn’t just about the ships. There are terrain rules for asteroids, planets, black holes, dust clouds and nebulas. And of course there are new weapons: web casters, webs, web breakers, and shield crackers.

This book is an expansion. You must have Klingon Armada Unity or Romulan Armada Unity. Play will be enhanced with the Starmada Core Rules.

This edition unifies the previous Nova and Admiralty editions, so there will no longer be Starmada fans who cannot game together. The new rules preserve the best of both previous systems, and add dynamic seeking weapons rules that do what seeking weapons are supposed to do: force the enemy to move out of key areas or shoot his way through a swarm of drones.

—description from the publisher
